data.name: AbsorptionIndDescription
data.value: The indicator analyzes footprint data and identifies absorption levels using the imbalance method, where significant order volumes indicate potential support or resistance zones.

data.name: AbsorptionThresholdDesc
data.value: Minimum distance between the close and the delta extremes to consider absorption

data.name: ABWMADescription
data.value: The Adaptive Binary Wave (ABW MA) indicator is a tool based on the Adaptive Moving Average (AMA) and Standard Deviation (StdDev) algorithms. "1" and "-1" signals are generated when the current AMA value exceeds the previous value by more than a specified percentage of up or down deviation, respectively; otherwise the signal is 0.

data.name: AccelMaxDescription
data.value: This property caps the maximum value that the acceleration factor can reach. This limitation ensures that the sensitivity of the SAR doesn't become excessive in highly trending markets.

data.name: AccelStartDescription
data.value: This property sets the initial acceleration factor for the Parabolic SAR calculation. It determines the rate at which the SAR moves in response to changes in price.

data.name: AccelStepDescription
data.value: The AccelStep property defines the step or increment by which the acceleration factor can increase.

data.name: AccessToMainFeaturesIsCurrentlyRestricted
data.value:
Access to main features is currently restricted
You can still trade crypto and receive limited data from other exchanges. To regain access to full functionality, renew your license in your Personal Area

data.name: AccountHasPositionWithSLTPText
data.value: Account {0} has an active position with SL/TP orders

data.name: AccountHasSLTPWithErrorsText
data.value: По счёту {0} есть SL/TP ордера с ошибками

data.name: AccountInfoDisplayDescription
data.value: Displays detailed account information on the chart

data.name: ACDCDescription
data.value: The AC DC Histogram indicator is a combined tool that includes elements of an oscillator and moving averages. The indicator is based on the difference between two moving averages using additional moving averages for smoothing, which allows you to identify changes in price dynamics. Four different periods are used for moving averages, which allows you to adapt the indicator to different market conditions.

data.name: ACDescription
data.value: The Bill Williams AC is a technical indicator that measures the acceleration or deceleration of the current market momentum. It is derived from the Awesome Oscillator (AO) and helps traders identify potential trend reversals or continuation patterns. The Bill Williams AC indicator consists of a histogram that fluctuates above and below a zero line, indicating the strength of the market momentum. Positive values above the zero line suggest bullish momentum, while negative values below the zero line indicate bearish momentum.

data.name: ACRDescription
data.value: The Average Candle Range (ACR) indicator calculates the average range of a candle from the beginning of the session to the current candle. The histogram shows changes in this average range.

data.name: ActivateByZeroPositionDescription
data.value: If this option is selected, then the strategy after adding will wait for the closing of all positions and cancelling current pending orders and then it is automatically activated, if when adding the strategy there are no open positions and no active orders, the strategy is activated immediately. If this option is not checked, then the strategy is activated immediately after adding.

data.name: ActivateHedgingModeMessage
data.value: With hedging mode enabled, the total volume of all open positions on the provider's account will be copied to the follower accounts according to the specified ratios. The actual positions on the follower accounts may differ from those in the same copying group on the provider's real exchange account.

data.name: ActiveVolumeDescription
data.value: The Active Volume indicator provides real-time data on cumulative buying (Ask) and selling (Bid) volumes at each price level, starting from the specified date.

data.name: AdaptiveBigTradesDescription
data.value: The Adaptive Big Trades indicator shows aggregated trades without applying any filters. The displayed data adapts to the current scale and visible area of the chart to show the top 10 volumes. The indicator receives real-time data. When loading data takes a lot of time, the message "Requesting data..." is displayed. During this time, the indicator’s markers will readjust until all data has been loaded.

data.name: AdaptiveRsiAverageDescription
data.value: Indicator Adaptive RSI Moving Average calculates and displays an adaptive moving average of the Relative Strength Index (RSI), which in turn is a function of the data specified in the input data. The indicator allows you to smooth both input data and RSI optionally.

data.name: AddedAnyInstrumentsToWatchlistByLimit
data.value: The first {0} instruments have been added to the watchlist, it is limited to {1} instruments.

data.name: ADDescription
AddingCopyGroupDescription
data.value: Assign a supplier account to add subscribers

data.name: ADFDescription
data.value: The Accumulation / Distribution Flow (ADF) indicator is an extended version of the Accumulation / Distribution (AD) indicator, provides information about the accumulation/distribution flow in real time using a moving average with a certain period. It uses volume and price data to assess whether assets are accumulating (upward) or distributing (downward).

data.name: ADRDescription
data.value: ADR (Average Daily Range) is an indicator that counts the average price range of the preset number of previous days and displays it in the current day.

data.name: ADXDescription
data.value: The ADX (Average Directional Index) is a tool designed to help traders assess the strength of a trend in the market. It is composed of two main components: the DX (Directional Index) and a smoothing mechanism provided by the WMA (Weighted Moving Average). ADX Line represents the overall trend strength. A rising ADX suggests a strengthening trend, while a falling ADX indicates a weakening trend.

data.name: AdxPeriodDescription
data.value: Adjusts the period used in the underlying ADX calculation.

data.name: ADXRDescription
data.value: The ADXR (Average Directional Movement Rating) is a technical indicator that enhances the ADX (Average Directional Index) by smoothing its values over a specified period. It helps traders gauge the strength of a trend more consistently by reducing short-term fluctuations.

data.name: AgreementMessage
data.value: Please accept or decline with following agreement.

data.name: AIAskInstructions
data.value: Enter your question or prompt for AI analysis of the chart:

data.name: AIAskPlaceholder
data.value: For example: Identify support and resistance levels, analyze trends, or explain market behavior...

data.name: AlertBackgroundDescription
data.value: This option is used to set the background color of alert messages. It allows users to customize the appearance of the background for notifications or alerts generated by the indicator.

data.name: AlertBeforeFileDescription
data.value: Specifies the sound file for the notification before the main alert.

data.name: AlertBeforeSecondsDescription
data.value: Sets the number of seconds before a new alert appears.

data.name: AlertFileDescription
data.value: Custom alert sound settings. The selected file must be in WAV format and located in the C:\Program Files (x86)\ATAS Platform\Sounds folder.

data.name: AlertFillColorDescription
data.value: Sets the background color of the alert window.

data.name: AlertFilterDescription
data.value: Notification when manually set volume level is reached.

data.name: AlertIntervalDescription
data.value: Time in seconds of next repeated alert

data.value: Notifier '{0}' was disabled due to sending data errors.

data.name: AlertPerBarDescription
data.value: If enabled, the alert will be triggered once during one bar.

data.name: AlertTextColorDescription
data.value: Sets the color of the text in the alert window.

data.name: AlignToSessionDescription
data.value: Disable to request all available data, enable to match data for ATAS instruments. Disabling this field will allow to see pre and post market tradings. Cache clean may be required.

data.name: AllGroupDescription
data.value:
A complete list of all available indicators in ATAS.

  • All indicators in one place, without filters by type or task.
  • A convenient starting point for searching and comparing tools.
  • Discover new solutions for your analysis.

data.name: AlligatorDescription
data.value: Alligator technical indicator represents nothing more than the usual combination of three smoothed moving averages (SMA) with different periods (13, 8 and 5), which shifted for various periods (8, 5 and 3, respectively). The author of this technical indicator uses the median price to build moving averages.

data.name: AllLevelsColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color of all levels.

data.name: AllowCustomStartPointDescription
data.value: Determines whether to use a custom starting point.

data.name: AllowPanelsReloadPrompt
data.value:
The platform requires to reload all panels to recover missing data.
This process may take time, especially with multiple open windows.
The panels are: {0}
Allow the reload?

data.name: AllPricesMenuDescription
data.value: Open a new All Prices module

data.name: AlphaMessage
data.value: Alpha version is a platform build which is intended for testers only and should not be used for any other purpose. They can be released several times a day, depending on how often the code is changed. Such builds are automatically generated, that is why you may be the first man to test a specific build. Sometimes even the basic functionality may be broken.

data.name: AlphaVersionDescription
data.value: The Alpha version is intended for testing new functionality

data.name: AMADescription
data.value: The Adaptive Moving Average (AMA) is a technical indicator designed to adapt to changing market conditions by adjusting its sensitivity to price movements. It aims to provide a smoothed moving average that responds more efficiently to shifts in market dynamics.

data.name: AnchoredVWAPObjDescription
data.value: This object is used to track the VWAP starting from a certain point (a specific bar) and can display different up and down standard deviation lines.

data.name: AnErrorOccurredWhileConnectingToServer
data.value: An error occurred while connecting to {0}: {1}

data.name: AngleDescription
data.value: This indicator calculates the angle between the horizontal chart axis and the straight line formed by the current price and the price of a given number of periods ago.

data.name: AngleObjDescription
data.value: Angle is a tool on financial charts for measuring the angle of trends, helping to assess the rate of price change and the strength of the trend.

data.name: AODescription
data.value: The Bill Williams Oscillator (Awesome Oscillator) is an indicator to measure the strength of market movement. This oscillator calculates the difference between a 34-period and a 5-period simple moving average (SMA). It is used to confirm current trends or to warn of possible reversals.

data.name: ApiKeyDescription
data.value: Api key from the admin panel

data.name: ApplingSLTPSettingsMayBeClosePositionMessage
data.value: Applying the settings may result in the position being closed for the following reasons:

data.name: ApplyActiveSLTPButtonDescription
data.value: Apply settings to an open position.

data.name: ApplyAndStartSLTPButtonDescription
data.value: Apply SL/TP settings and activate the strategy.

data.name: ApplyToAllWindowDescription
data.value: The volume change will be applied to all windows for which the {0} tool is set.

data.name: ApplyWatchButtonDescription
data.value: Apply SL/TP settings to an open position.

data.name: ApproximationFilterDescription
data.value: Defines the number of ticks by which the current price must approach the target level to trigger the alert.

data.name: AreaColorDescription
data.value: Sets the color for a specific area on the chart.

data.name: AreYouSureYouWantToDeleteElement
data.value: Are you sure you want to delete the selected item?

data.name: AreYouSureYouWantToDeleteThisTemplate
data.value: Are you sure you want to delete this template? This action cannot be undone.

data.name: AroonHighestDescription
data.value: Displays what percentage of time has passed since the last maximum.

data.name: AroonIndicatorDescription
data.value: The Aroon Indicator, consisting of Aroon Up and Aroon Down, gauges the time elapsed since the last high or low within a set period. This indicator helps assess the strength of a trend—closer to 100 indicates a stronger trend.

data.name: AroonLowestDescription
data.value: Displays what percentage of time has passed since the last minimum.

data.name: AroonOscillatorDescription
data.value: Aroon Oscillator is a technical indicator that measures the difference between Aroon Up and Aroon Down values. The Aroon Oscillator helps assess the strength and direction of a trend. The oscillator produces positive values when Aroon Up is greater than Aroon Down, indicating an uptrend, and negative values when Aroon Down is greater, indicating a downtrend. The closer the oscillator value is to +100, the stronger the uptrend, while values closer to -100 suggest a stronger downtrend.

data.name: DigitsAfterComma
data.value: Digits After The Decimal Point In Volume Values

data.name: VisibleProportion
data.value: Proportion By Visible Part Of The Chart

data.name: AskBidBarsDescription
data.value: Ask/Bid Volume Difference Bars - is a candlestick indicator that shows the dynamics of the selling and buying volume difference during one bar. The candlestick High is the maximum Delta during a bar, while the Low is the minimum Delta, and Close is the actual Delta.

data.name: AskVisualizationSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for trades closed at the Ask price.

data.name: AsRayDescription
data.value: Determines whether to represent the given lines as a ray.

data.name: ATRDescription
data.value: The Average True Range (ATR) indicator shows the degree of the price change for a specific period of time. In other words, it shows this asset volatility.

data.name: ATRLengthDescription
data.value: Length of the ATR calculation used for tolerance.

data.name: ATRMultiplierDescription
data.value: Multiplier applied to ATR to determine tolerance level.

data.name: ATRNDescription
data.value: ATR Normalized is a normalized version of the ATR indicator, used to measure the level of volatility as a % of the current price.

data.name: AtrPeriodDescription
data.value: Specifies the period of the ATR element.

data.name: AuthorDescription
data.value: Displays the author's name or pseudonym.

data.name: AuthorizationEmailHint
data.value: Enter the email you used when registering

data.name: AutoProportionDescription

data.name: AutoUpdateModulesFromPersonalAreaDescription
data.value: Automatically update modules (indicators, strategies and etc) from personal area on platform startup.

data.name: AutoUpdaterControlTitle
data.value: The version of {0} installed on this computer is {1}. The latest version is {2}. Listed below are the changes and improvements:

data.name: AvailableNewVersionMessage
data.value: New version {1} is available, you have version of the {0} installed on this computer {2}. Restart the platform to install the update.

data.name: AverageDeltaIndDescription
data.value: This indicator calculates a smoothed delta using EMA or SMA with a certain period.

data.name: AveragePriceBarDescription
data.value: Indicator Average Price for Bar calculates the average value based on the opening, high, low and closing prices, depending on the selected calculation mode.

data.name: AveragePriceCalculatedByPartTrades
data.value: The position price may be incorrect due to insufficient number of trades. The platform does not guarantee the price position correctness.

data.name: AveragePriceCannotBeCalculatedByTrades
data.value: The position price cannot be calculated by the platform due to the lack of trades received from the trading connection.

data.name: AverageVolumeSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the average volume elements.

data.name: AvgVolumeLineSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the average volume line.

data.name: AxisTextColorDescription
data.value: Text color for the indicator value label on the price axis.

data.name: BalanceOIDescription
data.value: On Balance Open Interest is a variation of the Open interest indicator. This indicator calculates and displays open interest on balance at a certain length of bars, taking into account price movement. An increase reflects an increase in the influence of buyers, a decrease - an increase in the influence of sellers.

data.name: BandsEnvelopeDescription
data.value: The Bands/Envelope indicator is a two-line band plotted on the chart, inside which the price chart is located. The indicator plotting method exactly repeats the plotting of the Bollinger Bands, except for the calculation of the distance to which the extreme lines are remote from the average line. While the Bollinger Bands are calculated using the standard deviation, in envelopes this distance is set manually in settings.

data.name: BarDifferenceDescription
data.value: The Bar Difference indicator is a tool for measuring the difference between the current value and the value on a specific previous bar. This difference is expressed in the number of ticks (units of price change). The indicator is useful for tracking price changes over a given period.

data.name: BarDirectionDescription
data.value: Determines the direction of the candles.

data.name: BarDirVsDeltaDivergenceDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display divergence, which occurs when the direction of the bar and the delta value are opposite: in the case of a bullish bar, the delta is negative, and in the case of a bearish bar, the delta is positive.

data.name: BarNumberingDescription
data.value: This indicator displays the bar sequence number at a certain interval.

data.name: BarRangeIndDescription
data.value: The indicator shows the price value corresponding to the size of each bar and identifies the highest price within the specified period of bars.

data.name: BarShiftDescription
data.value: Defines the number of bars for shift.

data.name: BarsLimitDescription
data.value: Defines the maximum number of bars for calculation.

data.name: BarsPatternDescription
data.value: The Bars Pattern indicator is a tool for highlighting and filtering candle patterns on the chart according to the specified settings. Those candles that combine all the established conditions for filtration are highlighted with a certain color.

data.name: BarsRangeDescription
data.value: This property is used to specify the number of candles that should be aggregated before the main calculation takes place.

data.name: BarTimerDescription
data.value: Bar Timer is a technical indicator which shows time remaining before candle closes.

data.name: BarVolumeFilterDescription
data.value: The Bar's Volume Filter indicator is a tool for filtering the volume of candles on the chart. It allows users to define criteria for showing or hiding candles based on volume and time.

data.name: BaseLinePeriodDescription
data.value: The period measured in bars for the base line.

data.name: BaseLineSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the base line.

data.name: BasicFootprintTemplateDescription
data.value: Visualize volumes inside the candles to detect large players accumulating positions. Such spikes can be used as entry signals, especially during VWAP level retests.

data.name: BearishColorDescription
data.value: Sets the color for elements with a bearish direction.

data.name: BearishFillColorDescription
data.value: Defines the background fill color of the bearish element.

data.name: BearishMissingStrengthColorDescription
data.value: Determines the color of the element displaying the missing strength of the bearish candle, i.e. bottom wick size.

data.name: BearishPartialStrengthColorDescription
data.value: Determines the color of the element displaying the partial strength of the bearish candle, i.e. from the high to the close of the candle.

data.name: BearishStrongColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color of the element representing the full strength of the bearish candle, i.e. from the high until the candle closes when the candle closes at the low.

data.name: BeforeReplayStartMustBeSelectInstrumentsMessage
data.value: At least one instrument must be selected to start history playback.

data.name: BelowChartDescription
data.value: Determines the order in which the object and chart candles are displayed: if true, the object is drawn under the candles; if false, candles are drawn under the object.

data.name: BestAskBGColorDescription
data.value: Sets the background color for the element displaying best ask volumes.

data.name: BestBidBGColorDescription
data.value: Sets the background color for the element displaying best bid volumes.

data.name: BetaMessage
data.value: The beta version has the latest features and fixes, which require testing and may have their own bugs or limitations. It was tested internally and is tested by the community of experienced ATAS users to make sure that there are no bugs in the stable version as much as possible.

data.name: BetaVersionDescription
data.value: Advanced functionality, but errors may occur (at your own risk)

data.name: BetweenColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color for elements displaying over-the-counter trades.

data.name: BidAskCalculationModeDescription
data.value: Allows to select the calculation mode, including Bid, Ask and BidAsk.

data.name: BidAskDescription
data.value: Bid Ask is an indicator that separates the volume for trades closed at Ask price (market buys) or Bid price (market sells). These histograms are built up, accordingly, above and below zero horizontal level.

data.name: BidAskImbalanceDescription
data.value: The template is a 30-minute cluster chart showing Bid-Ask overweights at adjacent price levels. Using the cluster settings, you can adjust the overweight proportions, set filters and colors with great flexibility.

data.name: BidAskImbalanceTemplateDescription
data.value: Reveal supply and demand imbalances. The imbalance chart helps pinpoint where buyers or sellers dominated — key to spotting short-term reversals.

data.name: BidAskTapeMenuDescription
data.value: Open a new Bid/Ask Tape module

data.name: BidAskVRDescription
data.value: Bid Ask Volume Ratio shows the ratio of buying and selling volumes, smoothed by a moving average to better track trends.

data.name: BidBGColorDescription
data.value: Sets the background color for the element displaying bid volumes.

data.name: BidVisualizationSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for trades closed at the Bid price.

data.name: BigTradesDescription
data.value: The template demonstrates the capabilities of the Big trades indicator. This indicator displays areas on the chart where large market buys and sells took place.

data.name: BigTradesIndDescription
data.value:
The Big Trades indicator displays large market buys and sells (which are visible in Smart Tape) on the chart single, the so-called aggressive trades that are executed in large volume at once. You can choose what volume you want to see and adjust it by bids and asks in the indicator settings. When installed, the indicator has default settings and these settings are not suitable for all instruments and time frames, so the indicator needs to be configured. The maximum number of days that can be calculated by the indicator is one week. That is, if the chart contains 100 days, the indicator will only calculate the orders for the last week.
The Autofilter changes dynamically, but only at the time of the first run, it does not change online. Autofilter may depend on the timeframe due to different number of days of history loaded by default. The indicator takes the last few days, and calculates aggregated orders. The filter is then chosen so that every day has about 10 splashes on the average. And if the indicator is only load for one day, it will consider the aggregated trades for the day and will display an average of ~10 splashes. If a week is loaded, the indicator will calculate orders for the week (including the current day) and will also display an average of ~10 splashes per day.

data.name: BionicCandleDescription
data.value: With this indicator, you can visualize the different strengths of the candles to identify future pullbacks within different movements. The key point of the Bionic candle is that it highlights the last move just before the close. If a candle closes at a high or low, then we interpret this candle as strong. The upper wick of a bullish candle and the lower wick of a bearish candle show the missing amount of candle power. Doji candles with wicks of different sizes indicate an initial manifestation of force, but its subsequent complete or partial repayment by the opposite force. Dojis with identical wicks indicate parity. Thus, a rollback from the high to the close of a bullish candle and from the low to the close of a bearish candle is the main criterion for the manifestation of the strength of the candle.

data.name: BitgetPostOnlyDescription
data.value: When post only is on, GTC options will be ignored. The Post-Only order will only be executed as a maker order. If it can be executed immediately as a taker order, it will be automatically canceled

data.name: BlockMADescription
data.value: Block Moving Average (BlockMA) indicator. This type of a moving average differs from others in that not one value, but a certain channel is calculated as a result. Also the ATR indicator is used in calculations.

data.name: BlockTextColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color of the text inside the TPO cell.

data.name: BollingerBandsBandwidthDescription
data.value: Bollinger Bands: Bandwidth is a derivative indicator of Bollinger Bands. It measures the difference between the upper and lower bands in percent. The Bandwidth value decreases when the difference between Bollinger Bands decreases and increases when the difference increases. Since the Bollinger Bands indicator is based on standard deviation, Bandwidth decrease reflects volatility weakening while Bandwidth increase reflects volatility strengthening.

data.name: BollingerBandsDescription
data.value: The Bollinger Bands (BB) indicator is a powerful tool in technical analysis, providing valuable insights into volatility, overbought/oversold conditions, and potential trend reversals.This indicator consists of a middle line, typically a simple moving average (SMA), and two outer bands that represent standard deviations from the SMA.

data.name: BollingerBandsPercentDescription
data.value: Bollinger Bands: Percentage - is calculated on the basis of the Bollinger Bands indicator. It is a percentage difference between the current price and Bollinger level.

data.name: BollingerSqueezeDescription
data.value: Bollinger Squeeze - this indicator is based on the Bollinger Bands and Keltner Channel indicators. The histogram reflects the relative difference between the width of the Keltner Channel and Bollinger Bands.

data.name: BollingerSqueezeV2Description
data.value: Bollinger Squeeze 2 is a variation of the Bollinger Squeeze indicator. Besides the Bollinger Bands and the Keltner Channel in the Bollinger Squeeze 2, the Momentum and Exponential Moving Average indicators are used.

data.name: BollingerSqueezeV3Description
data.value: Bollinger Squeeze 3 is a variation of the Bollinger Squeeze indicator. The ATR and StdDev indicators are used when calculating.

data.name: BOPDescription
data.value: The Balance of Power (BOP) indicator assesses the strength of buyers relative to sellers in the market. It is calculated by determining the ratio of the close price to the range between the high and low prices of a given period. The BOP value is then smoothed using a Simple Moving Average (SMA) to better represent the balance between buying and selling pressure.

data.name: BorderColorDescription
data.value: Border color of candles and bars in the indicator.

data.name: BorderStyleDescription
data.value: Sets the border line style of the displayed element.

data.name: BorderWidthPixelDescription
data.value: Sets the width of the border of the displayed element (in pixels).

data.name: BottomBandDscription
data.value: Describes the lower boundary of the range.

data.name: BottomChannelSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the bottom channel line.

data.name: BreakevenHelpText
data.value:
Breakeven
Allows you to move the SL order when the specified profit value for the position is reached. Moving the SL order to breakeven occurs only once.
Parameters:
• Breakeven - the profit value at which the SL order should be moved to another level
• Offset - the offset from the average price of the position at which the SL order should be moved after reaching the specified profit value.

data.name: BreakevenTooltip
data.value:
Allows moving SL order when specified profit value is reached for the position. Moving SL order to breakeven triggers only once.
Parameters: • Breakeven — profit value at which SL order should be moved to another level • Offset — offset from the average position price to which SL order should be moved after reaching the specified profit value

data.name: BreakevenTrailingRequiresSL
data.value: Breakeven and Trailing become active only after enabling the SL checkbox

data.name: BreakoutRetestPeriodDescription
data.value: Number of bars to look back for identifying pivot points.

data.name: BrokenBearishDescription
data.value: Defines the color for a broken bearish element.

data.name: BrokenBullishDescription
data.value: Defines the color for a broken bullish element.

data.name: BullishColorDescription
data.value: Sets the color for elements with a bullish direction.

data.name: BullishFillColorDescription
data.value: Defines the background fill color of the bullish element.

data.name: BullishMissingStrengthColorDescription
data.value: Determines the color of the element displaying the missing strength of the bullish candle, i.e. top wick size.

data.name: BullishPartialStrengthColorDescription
data.value: Determines the color of the element displaying the partial strength of the bullish candle, i.e. from the low to the close of the candle.

data.name: BullishStrongColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color of the element representing the full strength of the bullish candle, i.e. from the low until the candle closes when the candle closes at the high.

data.name: BuySellModeDescription
data.value: Sets one of the available buy/sell modes.

data.name: BuySellPowerPeriodDescription
data.value: Defines the period value for the average moving buy/sell power.

data.name: BuySellPowerSettingsDescription
data.value: Settings for displaying the dominance of buy/sell power.

data.name: BuySignalColorDescription
data.value: Sets the color of the element representing the buy signal.

data.name: BuySignalSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the buy signal.

data.name: BuyTradeColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color for the element displaying buy trades.

data.name: BuyTradeLineColorDescription
data.value: Specifies the color used to display buy trade lines and markers.

data.name: BWMADescription
data.value: Bill Williams Moving Average technical indicator shows the average price value of an instrument for a certain time period. In contrast to Simple Moving Average, there is no mathematical averaging of the instrument price for a given period of time. Only the current price value and the previous indicator value are used in the calculation. The indicator is calculated according to the formula: BWMA(t) = (1-1/N)*BWMA(t-1)+CLOSE(t)/N

data.name: BybitAccountTypeDescription
data.value: Use MainNet, Demo or TestNet account

data.name: BybitUnifiedChangeMarginTypeRestricted

data.name: CalculateLastBarDescription

data.name: CalculateOnBarCloseDescription
data.value: This setting controls when the indicator calculations are performed. When enabled, the indicator will only calculate and update its values once a bar is completely formed and closed.

data.name: CamarillaPivotsDescription
data.value: The Camarilla Pivots indicator displays historical data about Camarilla pivot levels on the chart. Camarilla reversal levels were developed in 1989 by Nick Scott. These levels are used as major support and resistance levels in intraday trading. These levels are derived from the high, low and close prices of the previous session.

If this option is disabled, accordingly all pending orders (Stop-Loss, Take-Profit) of this particular strategy will remain active, but independent from each other and from the position.

data.name: CAVDescription
data.value: Cumulative Adjusted Value calculates the total sum of divergences between the price and its Exponential Moving Average. Summation values are calculated by the following formula: AdjValt(X,n)=Xt−EMAt(X,n)

data.name: CBIDescription
data.value: Connie Brown Composite Index – this indicator eliminates the problem of discrepancies in the RSI indicator. It is calculated as the Momentum sum from the RSI value and smoothed RSI value.

data.name: CButtonTooltip
data.value: Custom price level height is enabled. Click to disable.

data.name: CCIDescription
data.value: The Commodity Channel Index (CCI) is a tool that measures the current price level relative to the average price level over a given period and helps determine when an asset is overbought or oversold. The Commodity Channel Index (CCI) helps to notice the weakening or completion of a trend and a change in its direction.

data.name: CenterAlignDescription
data.value: If enabled, aligns text to the center.

data.name: CFODescription
data.value: Chande Forecast Oscillator (CFO) – an oscillator that reflects the difference between the expected and real prices. It is calculated as the difference in percent between the expected (identified by x-period of linear regression) and current instrument prices. The oscillator is above zero when the forecasted value is above the current price. Otherwise, it is below zero when the forecasted value is below the current price.

data.name: ChaikinOscillatorDescription
data.value: The Chaikin Oscillator is the difference between the 3-day and 10-day EMAs of the accumulation distribution line. Like other momentum indicators, this indicator is designed to anticipate directional changes in the accumulation distribution line by measuring the momentum behind the movements.

data.name: ChandeMomentumDescription
data.value: The Chande Momentum Oscillator is designed to assess the momentum of a financial instrument by calculating the difference between the sum of positive price changes and the sum of negative price changes over a specified period. It is particularly useful for identifying the strength and direction of a trend.

data.name: ClusterAnalysisDescription
data.value:
In front of you is a book that will become your guide and reliable assistant in the world of trade and analysis on financial markets. This book is dedicated to one of the most effective and high-tech analysis tools - cluster or footprint analysis. This tool allows you to penetrate the essence of market processes, see and interpret details that remain unnoticed when using classical approaches. We will start with the analysis of the basic principles of building clusters and footprint analysis, as well as consider what value their use can bring to your trading. The next stage is understanding and interpreting footprint charts, various types of footprints: Bid/Ask, Delta, Volume, and Volume Profile. But what does this give in practice? The practical application of what we will study will be discussed in the section on trading approaches based on footprint. Here we will dive into such aspects as Bid/Ask imbalance, completed and unfinished auctions, trend trading from clusters of volumes, reversal trading, and many others.
You will also find detailed information on setting up clusters in the ATAS platform. We will discuss basic cluster settings, proportionality parameters, maximum candle volume, various filters, and other parameters.
Our goal is to make this seemingly complex tool accessible and understandable to you so that you can confidently use it in your trading practice. Be prepared for your understanding of the market to undergo significant changes and reach a new level.

data.name: ClusterSearchAutofilterDescription
data.value: Enables dynamic adjustment of the indicator's filters to suit a specified instrument. When the auto-filter is activated, the parameters for the maximum and minimum filters are disabled

data.name: ClusterSearchDescription
data.value: The indicator effectively analyzes cluster data (footprint). It boasts a wide range of customizable filters, allowing users to flexibly approach various market scenarios. The filters include settings for analyzing trade volumes, time frames, price levels, and other criteria, making it an ideal tool for identifying cluster patterns and optimizing trading strategies.

data.name: ClustersModeDescription
data.value: Determines whether the chart is displayed in cluster mode.

data.name: ClusterStatisticDescription
data.value: The indicator allows getting full information about the structure of each bar.

data.name: ClusterStatisticFullDescription
data.value:
The template allows getting full information about the structure of each bar.

  • The cluster mode is presented as a histogram which is based on volume proportion. It allows you to see levels with the highest trading volumes.
  • Clusters colored by Delta allow you to see levels with the greatest predominance of buyers or sellers.
  • The Cluster Search indicator allows you to see the total values within each candle. If necessary, some options can be disabled.

data.name: CMFDescription
data.value: Сhaikin Money Flow (CMF) - is a technical analysis indicator which is used to measure the volume of money flow over a certain period of time. The money flow volume is an indicator used to measure pressure on buying and selling securities over one period. The CMF then sums up the money flow for a user-defined period. You can use any period, but the most popular periods are 20 or 21 days. The value of Chaikin’s money flow is arranged 1 and -1. CMF can be used further to quantify changes in pressure on buying and selling and can help anticipate future changes.

data.name: CMODescription
data.value: The Chaikin Money Oscillator is an indicator derived from the Accumulation/Distribution line (ADL). It calculates two Exponential Moving Averages with different lengths applied to the ADL. The oscillator value is obtained by subtracting the longer-term EMA from the shorter-term EMA. This provides a measure of ADL momentum, represented by a line fluctuating between positive and negative values. Monitoring these fluctuations helps anticipate trend changes, as shifts in momentum often precede changes in trend.

data.name: CMSDescription
data.value: The Clear Method Swing Line indicator is to transform accumulated historical data and makes it possible to detect various features and patterns in price dynamics that are invisible to the naked eye. The algorithm behind CMS involves intricate calculations based on price highs, lows, and momentum conditions.

data.name: ColorBarHighLowIndDescription
data.value: This indicator colors candles whose level range has moved up or down. If the low of the candle becomes lower than the previous one, and the high becomes higher than the previous candle, then the average color is selected.

data.name: ColorBarOpenCloseDescription
data.value: This indicator paints the candles with selected colors depending on their direction.

data.name: CoppockCurveDescription
data.value: The Coppock Curve is an indicator designed to identify changes in a financial instrument's long-term trend. It is composed of a Weighted Moving Average (WMA) of the sum of two Rate of Change (ROC) calculations, one with a shorter period (11) and the other with a longer period (14). The ROC measures the percentage change in price over a specified period.

data.name: COTGroupDescription
data.value:
Positions of major players — now at your fingertips.
The "COT" category includes:

  • Weekly reports from the CFTC on positions held by hedgers, managers, and speculators.
  • A long-term view of market sentiment and fundamental trends.
  • Unique insight into actual capital allocation.

Analyze the behavior of those who move the markets and build your strategy based on facts.

data.name: CotHighDescription
data.value: This indicator is a cumulative delta that resets to zero whenever a new price high is reached on the chart.

data.name: CotIndexDescription
data.value: COT Index is the most popular, efficient and simple indicator for identifying extreme market overbying and overselling points. It is calculated on the basis of the trader net position data, taken from the Commitment Of Traders (COT) reports, using the Stochastic oscillator formula. The indicator values from 0% to 20% and from 80% to 100% point to the moments of the market extreme overbuying or overselling, sending a signal about a frequent price reversal after reaching these levels. Net positions of commercial, non-commercial and non-reported traders, as well as the Open Interest, are used as a basis for calculations.

data.name: CotIndicatorDescription
data.value: CFTC Net Positions is a weekly report of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), which reflects the difference between the total volume of long and short positions, which exist in the market. The report covers American futures markets (Chicago and New York exchanges) only. So, the indicator COT Net Positions presents the net volume of long positions in the United States. The indicator reflects positions of funds and managers (Commercial), major players (Large Speculators) and minor players (Small Traders).

data.name: CotOiDescription
data.value: The COT Open Interest is visually represented on the chart as a line, illustrating the overall volume of open positions for a specific financial instrument at a given point in time.

data.name: COTReportsDescription
data.value: Analyze positions of major market participants using Commitments of Traders reports. The COT chart helps understand the behavior of market makers, hedgers, and speculators — essential for mid- to long-term trading.

data.name: CotWilliamsIndexDescription
data.value: COT Williams Index is a variation of the COT Index indicator. The main difference is replacement of the weighted position volumes in the index calculation with its percentage of the total position.

data.name: CreateFirstPlaybookHint
data.value: Click the "Add Playbook" button to create your first playbook

data.name: CreatePlaybookHelperText
data.value: Add a new playbook to structure your trading setups

data.name: CriticalMarketDataLatencyMessage
data.value: Critical delay of Market data at {0:F0} ms.

data.name: CriticalTimeSinceLastMarketDataReceivedMessege
data.value: Market data has not been received for a critically long time; the last update was {0}.

data.name: CrossLineObjDescription
data.value: Cross line is a tool consisting of two intersecting lines (horizontal and vertical) used to indicate a specific price and time on an asset price chart.

data.name: CrossMarginModeDescription
data.value: All cross positions under the same margin asset share the same asset cross margin balance. In the event of liquidation, your assets full margin balance along with any remaining open positions under the asset may be forfeited.

data.name: CryptoMetricsGroupDescription
data.value:
Understand the market from the inside out.
The "Crypto Metrics" category includes:

  • On-chain and exchange metrics to assess real network activity and capital flows.
  • Insights into investor behavior, sentiment, and long-term vs short-term positioning.
  • Early signals of accumulation, distribution, and trend shifts based on fundamental crypto data.

Make more informed trading decisions by combining price action with the underlying metrics that drive the crypto market.

data.name: CryptoQuestionForOpenPending
data.value: Do you want to place {0} order at {1:v c}, volume {2:v c}?

data.name: CumDeltaLineColorDescription
data.value: Specifies the color to display the cumulative delta line.

data.name: CumDeltaLineWidthDescription
data.value: Defines the line width for displaying the high/low line.

data.name: CumulativeDailyVolumeDescription
data.value: This indicator displays the cumulative volume over a daily period.

data.name: CumulativeDeltaDescription
data.value: Cumulative Volume Delta (CVD) means cumulative delta for a certain period of time. If classic Delta shows the difference between market buys and sells for each bar separately, Cumulative Delta displays the entire dynamics of balance of market buys and sells starting from a certain point of time (from the beginning of a trading session or contract).

data.name: CumulativeModeAskColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color of the element that displays the ask level in the cumulative trade mode.

data.name: CumulativeModeBidColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color of the element that displays the bid level in the cumulative trade mode.

data.name: CumulativeTradesModeDescription
data.value: A mode in which only cumulative transactions are used in calculations.

data.name: CurrentDayLowDescription

data.name: CurrentDayMedianDescription

data.name: CurrentPriceDescription

data.name: CustomGroupDescription
data.value:
Create unique tools tailored to your needs.
The "Custom" category includes:

  • User-created indicators for specific use cases.
  • Complete freedom in configuration and calculation logic.
  • A playground for testing your own ideas and hypotheses.

Perfect for advanced traders and those who like to go beyond the standard.

data.name: DailyHighLowDescription

data.name: DailyLinesDescription

data.name: DaysBackWithVolumesDescription
data.value: How many days from today and backward to load the full volume profiles. More days = slower the chart loading but more data.

data.name: DaysFixedTooltip
data.value: Activating this feature allows you to change the timeframe without resetting the specified count of days to the standard values.

data.name: DaysLookBackDescription
data.value: Refers to the number of previous days taken into account when calculating an indicator.

data.name: DaysToSaveDescription
data.value: Defines the number of days to save data.

data.name: DecreasedMomentumSettingsDescription
data.value: Rendering settings for the element representing the reduced Momentum value.

data.name: DefaultInstructions
data.value: ATAS platform gives you an opportunity to simultaneously connect to several trade accounts and/or to receive Exchange online data. We made a table with a description of the supported features for each connection. Click here to open it.

data.name: DefaultMinimizedColorDescription
data.value: Color for filling dom when chart scale is small

data.name: DefaultTemplateNameErrorMessage
data.value: This name is used for the default template.

data.name: DegreeOf2PeriodDescription
data.value: Represents the period expressed by raising the number 2 to a certain power.

data.name: DelayedInstrumentDescription
data.value: Market data for the instrument arrives with a 15-minute delay.

data.name: DeleteAccountConfirmation
data.value: Are you sure you want to delete this account from statistics? The data will be permanently deleted.

data.name: DeleteLayoutWithPanelsMessage
data.value: There are panels that forbid the removal of the layout. Delete the layout?

data.name: DeletePlaybookConfirmation
data.value: Are you sure you want to delete this playbook?

data.name: DeleteStartPointKeyFilterDescription
data.value: If this field is active, it allows you to define a hotkey for deleting the previously set starting point.

data.name: DeleteWorkspaceMessage
data.value: Do you want to delete workspace {0}?

data.name: DeleteWorkspaceWithPanelsMessage
data.value: There are panels that forbid the removal of the workspace. Delete the workspace?

data.name: DeltaColoredCandlesDescription
data.value: The indicator calculates the total delta for a certain period and determines how close this value is to the set maximum. In this case, the candles are painted in accordance with the color scheme.

data.name: DeltaColoredDescription
data.value: Determines whether to color elements according to their delta value.

data.name: DeltaDescription
data.value: This indicator Delta shows the delta value for each bar, the difference between market buys and sells.

data.name: DeltaFilterDescription
data.value: This property is used to set a filter on the delta for a specific price level. If the value is positive, the filter will only allow price levels where the delta is greater than or equal to the value. If the value is negative, the filter will only allow price levels where the delta is less than or equal to the value.

data.name: DeltaImbalanceDescription
data.value: This property filters price levels based on the percentage of buy (Ask) or sell (Bid) volume relative to the total volume at the level. If the value is positive, the filter will only allow price levels where the percentage of ask volume is greater than or equal to the value. If the value is negative, the filter will only allow price levels where the percentage of bid volume is greater than or equal to the value.

data.name: DeltaStrengthDescription
data.value: Delta Strength marks bars where the delta is not less than the indicated percentage of the bar's maximum delta.

data.name: DeltaTurnaroundDescription
data.value: Delta Turnaround displays potential pivot points in the chart. Conditions for an up signal: there are two consecutive bearish candles, and the third candle is bullish, while the delta of the bullish candle is positive and its low is less than or equal to the low of the previous candle. Conditions for a down signal: there are two consecutive bullish candles, and the third candle is bearish, while the delta of the bearish candle is negative and its high is greater than or equal to the low of the previous candle.

data.name: DeltaTypeDescription
data.value: Specifies the delta type: positive, negative, or both.

data.name: DEMADescription
data.value: Double Exponential Moving Average is a moving average variation, which is calculated by the following formula: DEMA(t) = 2*EMA(x(t)) - EMA(EMA(x(t)))

data.name: DemandDescription
data.value: Demand Index combines price and volume in a special way, so it often serves as a leading indicator of price changes. The Demand index was developed by James Sibbet. James Sibbet formulated six rules for interpreting the demand index: - A divergence between the demand index and prices signals an upcoming price reversal. - Extremely high values of the demand index often precede the formation of new price highs (the index is a leading indicator). - The combination of a new price high with a lower peak of the demand index usually falls on an important price peak (the index is a synchronous indicator). - Crossing of the zero level by the demand index usually occurs after a price trend reversal and serves as its confirmation (the index is a confirming indicator). - If the demand index constantly fluctuates around zero, the current price trend has weak potential and will not last long. - A significant long-term divergence between prices and the demand index indicates an important market top or bottom.

data.name: DeMarkerDescription
data.value: The DeMarker (DeM) indicator quantifies the relationship between recent price highs and lows to assess the market's momentum. It calculates two values, DeMax and DeMin, based on the difference between consecutive high and low prices. These values are then smoothed using simple moving averages (SMA). The DeMarker indicator is computed as the ratio of the smoothed DeMax to the sum of smoothed DeMax and DeMin. This ratio reflects the market's momentum, with higher values indicating stronger upward momentum. The indicator helps traders identify potential overbought or oversold conditions in the market.

data.name: DemoTariffsHeader
data.value: The demo period is over. Pay for a subscription and keep making money.

data.name: DenyChangeInstrumentWhilePlayingReplay
data.value: You cannot open a new module and/or change the instrument in the module while the history is being played.

data.name: DepthLevelsCountDescription
data.value: Number of levels in the DOM, 0 - unlimited. The fewer levels are requested, the faster they are processed.

data.name: DetectNotLoadedModules
data.value: Modules found that were not loaded. Please select the actions to apply to these modules.

data.name: DetectPanelsWithoutLayout
data.value: Windows the layout of which is unknown are found. Please select the actions to be applied to these windows.

data.name: DeTrendedDescription
data.value: The DeTrended Price Oscillator is designed to reveal short-term price fluctuations by removing the trend component. It does this by calculating a simple moving average (SMA) and then subtracting it from the current price value. The length of the SMA is determined by the user-defined period. This indicator helps traders identify deviations from the overall trend, providing insights into potential reversals or short-term price movements in the market.

data.name: DeTrendedDiDescription
data.value: The indicator Detrended Oscillator - DiNapoli is a tool for calculating market overbought/oversold zones. By subtracting the simple moving average (SMA) value from the current price, this line reflects the deviation between the current price and its SMA for the specified period.

data.name: DeviationPercentageDescription
data.value: Determines the level of difference between the current value and its previous values, measured as a percentage.

data.name: DeviationRangeDescription
data.value: Represents the amount by which the upper and lower bands deviate from the current price.

data.name: DeviationValueDescription
data.value: Sets or gets the value for the given deviation.

data.name: DiamondObjDescription
data.value: This drawing object allows you to display a diamond on the chart.

data.name: DigitsAfterComma
data.value: Digits after the decimal point in volume values

data.name: DigitsAfterCommaDescription
data.value: Digits after the decimal point in volume values

data.name: DIMinusDescription
data.value: Negative Directional Indicator (DI-) indicates the price decline.

data.name: DINegIndDescription
data.value: The indicator Negative Directional Index (DI-) displays the strength of a downtrend using a weighted moving average (WMA) and ATR.

data.name: DIPlusDescription
data.value: Positive Directional Indicator (DI+) indicates the price rise.

data.name: DIPosIndDescription
data.value: The indicator Positive Directional Index (DI+) displays the strength of an uptrend using a weighted moving average (WMA) and ATR.

data.name: DisableAutoLoad3rdPartyModulesDescription
data.value: If checked, automatic updating of modules (indicators, strategies, etc.) from the personal account will not be offered when launching the platform.

data.name: DisableDebugMessage
data.value:
You have debug logs enabled. You may have enabled them when you contacted technical support.
If the issue is relevant, you can leave it as is.
If not, we recommend disabling them.

data.name: DisableLayoutsOnReplayMessage
data.value: In Replay mode, the functions for managing work layers are not available.

data.name: DisableSLTPButtonDescription
data.value: Save SL/TP settings and turn off the strategy.

data.name: DisableSLTPForCopingText
data.value: To start copying, turn off all SL/TP orders.

data.name: DiscrepancyDetectedDescription
data.value: Change follower volume manually or confirm without changes

data.name: DispersionDescription
data.value: The Dispersion indicator is a technical analysis tool used to assess the dispersion or spread of price values around a simple moving average (SMA). It provides insights into the volatility or variability of price movements within a specified period.

data.name: DisplayClusterBorderDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display the border of the clusters.

data.name: DisplayFromZeroDescription
data.value: When enabled, show values ranging from 0 to the maximum if the minimum is positive, and from 0 to the minimum if the maximum is negative.

data.name: DisplayHighLowLineDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display the element that represents the high/low line.

data.name: DisplayLabelChartBottomDescription
data.value: Allows you to display a label at the bottom of the chart.

data.name: DisplayLabelClustersModeOnlyDescription
data.value: If enabled, the label is displayed only in cluster mode

data.name: DisplaySMADescription
data.value: Determines whether to display an element that represents a moving average.

data.name: DivergenceDotsDescription
data.value: Shows divergence markers on the price chart when bar direction and delta are opposite.

data.name: DivergenceBarsDescription
data.value: Highlights divergence bars in the Delta panel when bar direction and delta are opposite.

data.name: DividedByPriceDescription
data.value: 1 Divided by Price - this indicator reflects the reverse chart. The candlestick values are equal to 1/Initial Price.

data.name: DivisorDescription
data.value: The divisor is used to bring the result into a convenient format by dividing it by a certain coefficient.

data.name: DMIDescription
data.value: The Dynamic Momentum Index (DMI) indicator is designed to assess the strength and direction of a prevailing trend. It achieves this by calculating the Relative Strength Index (RSI) dynamically based on certain parameters. It utilizes the standard deviation and simple moving average to assess volatility and smooth the data.

data.name: DmIndexDescription
data.value: Directional Movement Index consists of a plus directional indicator (+DI) and a minus directional indicator (-DI). A preponderance of (+ DI) or (-DI) indicates an uptrend or a downtrend, respectively.

data.name: DmOscillatorDescription
data.value: Directional Movement Oscillator consists of a plus directional indicator (+DI) and a minus directional indicator (-DI). It is calculated as the difference between indicators (+DI) and (-DI). The oscillator’s positive values show bullish trends, while negative ones show bearish trends.

data.name: DojiCandleBearDescription
data.value: Defines the color of an element that displays a Doji candle with a top wick but no bottom wick, indicating sufficient seller opposition.

data.name: DojiCandleBullDescription
data.value: Defines the color of an element that displays a Doji candle with a lower wick, but without an upper wick, indicating sufficient buyer opposition.

data.name: DojiEmptyColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color of an element that displays a Doji candle without wicks, indicating that there is no struggle between buyers and sellers.

data.name: DOMDescription
data.value: The indicator displays the current bid and ask levels and their volumes in real time.

data.name: DomLevelsDescription
data.value: The Dom Levels indicator allows to display data changes in the DOM. The indicator works only online and starts building markers from the moment you have added it on the chart. The minimum/maximum volume, basic ask/bid colors, and colors of bids, and asks which are higher than the maximum volumes set in the indicator. If the limit level volume in the DOM is higher than the minimum, but lower than the maximum volume, the price level is colored proportionally to the volume. If the limit level volume in the DOM is higher than the maximum volume, the price level is colored with the color of the maximum volumes. It is better to use this indicator in a tick chart with a value = 1, where each bar (tick) consists of a separate aggregated trade, or in the Cumulative Trades chart with a possibility to set your own volume filter values. Besides, when hovering over the indicator marker, it is possible to see data values in the DOM at a particular level at a certain moment.

data.name: DOMMaxFilterDescription
data.value: Determines the maximum number of market depth levels.

data.name: DOMPercentDescription
data.value: The initial ratio of the volume and the DOM, from which the divergence will be determined.

data.name: DomPowerDescription
data.value:
The indicator shows ratio of buyers and sellers in the DOM. It displays: - values of total Asks and total Bids on the each candle - range of variation of Asks and Bids delta for each candle (Delta = Bid-Ask in this indicator).
The blue range (the delta) in the indicator is the range of changes of the amount of bids and asks.That is, the difference between the amount of asks and the amount of bids is taken. Also it changes during the formation of the bar, the range displays the maximum and the minimum values of this indicator.

data.name: DOMProDescription
data.value: A universal DOM template focused on speed, precision, and clarity. Ideal for active intraday trading: compact interface, intuitive order control, and real-time volume and liquidity visualization. Everything you need — right at your fingertips.

data.name: DomStrengthDescription
data.value: The DOM Strength indicator shows the ratio of the traded volume for the specified period to the current volume in the DOM. Markers, the color of which indicates the current ratio of completed trades and the DOM, are displayed on top of the Delta indicator. The markers on the top show the ratio of buys to the ask volume in the DOM, while the markers on the bottom show the ratio of sells to the bid volume.

data.name: DOMTradingDarkTemplateDescription
data.value: Track market aggression in real-time: who’s hitting the market orders and how aggressively. This can signal a potential level breakout.

data.name: DOMTradingLightTemplateDescription
data.value: An alternative for those who prefer light interfaces — the same accurate analysis of market aggression in a visually comfortable format.

data.name: DonchianDescription
data.value: The Donchian channel is an indicator formed by taking the highest highs and the lowest lows over the last n periods. The area between high and low is a channel for the selected period. Donchian channel is an indicator to monitor the volatility of market prices. If the price is stable, the channel will be relatively narrow Donchian. If the price fluctuates, Donchian channel will be wider.

data.name: DoNotShowAboveChartDescription
data.value: If enabled, does not display elements in the bar rendering area.

data.name: DoNotShowDialogAgain
data.value: Do not show this dialog again

data.name: DontAskAgainAboutClosePanelsDescription
data.value: Don't ask for confirmation to close modules anymore.

data.name: DontAskAgainInTables
data.value: Do not ask for confirmation in tables again

data.name: DontAskTodayAboutClosePanelsDescription
data.value: Do not ask for confirmation to close modules today.

data.name: DotObjDescription
data.value: This drawing object allows you to display a dot on the chart.

data.name: DoubleStochasticBressertDescription
data.value: Double Stochastic Bressert is an indicator based on Double Stochastic. It is calculated as an EMA of the Double Stochastic values.

data.name: DoubleStochasticDescription
data.value: Double Stochastic is an indicator based on Stochastic. It is calculated as a Stochastic value of the already calculated Stochastic. This solution helps to remove a lot of false signals.

data.name: DownAlertFileFilterDescription
data.value: When activated, an alert is triggered in the presence of a downstream signal, utilizing the specified sound file name.

data.name: DownAreaSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the falling area.

data.name: DownArrowObjDescription
data.value: This drawing object allows you to display a down arrow on the chart.

data.name: DrawAbovePriceDescription
data.value: Determines whether the visual elements are drawn above or below the price data on a chart. When enabled, it overlays the indicator's graphics over the price plot.

data.name: DrawingObjectGlobalDescription
data.value: Determines whether the object is global.

data.name: DrawingObjectSizeDescription
data.value: Sets or gets the size value for this drawing object.

data.name: DrawingPositionObjDescription
data.value: This drawing object is designed to create and manage graphical elements that display trading positions and visualize data, including profit and loss zones and price tags. Both long and short positions are supported.

data.name: DrawLinesDescription
data.value: Enables or disables the display of boundary lines on the chart.

data.name: DtOscillatorDescription
data.value: DT Oscillator – the indicator displays a stochastic oscillator based on the Relative Strength Index (RSI) instead of price, which is the main method of the stochastic oscillator calculation.

data.name: DuplicateConnectorCredentials
data.value: A connector with the same credentials is already connected. Use the existing connection or enter different login credentials.

data.name: DuplicatedMarketDataSourceWarning
data.value: You have two or more connectors receiving data for the same instruments. Please note that this can cause data to be duplicated! To avoid this, keep one connector as the primary source of quotes.

data.name: DXDescription
data.value: The DX (Directional Movement Index) indicator shows how strong the trend is in the market. It determines this by looking at positive movement (DI+) and negative movement (DI-). It then compares how strong these movements are relative to each other. The indicator shows whether there is a strong trend and in what direction.

data.name: DxFeedLimitedOrderBookDescription
data.value: Limit the number of levels in the DOM on the server, this allows to reduce the amount of processed data and increase the speed of their processing.

data.name: DynamicLevelsChannelDescription
data.value: The indicator dynamically determines and shows the values of the POC level and the channel formed by the VAH and VAL levels, taking into account the period selected by the user. Based on price changes relative to these levels, signals of increasing selling or buying pressure are generated.

data.name: DynamicLevelsDescription
data.value: The indicator uses volume information to dynamically calculate and visualize key levels such as VAH, VAL, POC. *Note! The indicator calculates based on the data loaded on the chart. That is, if you upload 5 days to the chart and select a month for the indicator period, it will not calculate the data for the month, but only for 5 days.

data.name: DynamicPocObjDescription
data.value: This drawing object is designed to dynamically display the Point of Control (POC) level in a selected area of ​​the chart. The POC represents the price level with the highest trading volume over a specified period. The object is automatically updated in real time as market data changes.

data.name: EditInExtendedSettingsTooltip
data.value: To edit settings while position is open, use extended settings

data.name: ElderRayDescription
data.value:
Elder Ray tracks the bulls’ and bears’ relative strength by measuring the deviation value of the bar high and low prices from the average price value.
The Elder Ray indicator was developed by Alexander Elder and was named so by analogy with x-rays. It shows bulls’ and bears’ strength structure. Elder Ray is a combination of moving averages, which follow the trend, and two oscillators.

data.name: ElementDisplayModeDescription
data.value: Allows you to select one of the available display modes for this element.

data.name: ElementScaleValueDescription
data.value: Defines the scale value for the element.

data.name: ElliotWaveObjectDescription
data.value: The Elliott Wave drawing object is designed to create and manage graphical elements that display market waves based on the Elliott Wave Theory. It helps visualize impulse (1-5) and corrective (A-B-C) wave structures, enabling traders to identify potential market trends and reversals. The tool supports customization of wave labels and appearance for better clarity and analysis across different time frames.

data.name: EllipsObjDescription
data.value: This drawing object allows you to display a ellips on the chart.

data.name: EMADescription
data.value: EMA (Exponential Moving Average) indicator is an exponential moving average that is calculated by adding a certain share of the current closing price to the previous moving average value. When exponential moving averages are used, last closing prices are of more importance.

data.name: EmailFromDescription
data.value: From whom the e-mail will be sent.

data.name: EmailSmtpLoginDescription
data.value: The login for authorization on the SMTP-server.

data.name: EmailSmtpPasswordDescription
data.value: Password for authorization on the SMTP server.

data.name: EmailSmtpServerDescription
data.value: Specify the address of the SMTP-server.

data.name: EmailToDescription
data.value: To whom the e-mail will be sent.

data.name: EMALineSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the EMA line.

data.name: EMAPeriodDescription
data.value: Sets the period of the exponential moving average (EMA)

data.name: EMVDescription
data.value: Ease of Movement (or Arms' Ease of Movement Value, also known as EMV for Arms' Ease of Movement Value) is a technical indicator that is designed to show how easily price changes occur in the market. It analyzes the relationship between price changes and trading volume, providing information about how freely the market price can move.

data.name: EnableCrossTradingDescription
data.value: Enables the ability to place trades on a different instrument than the one displayed in the chart or Smart DOM. When enabled, the platform allows you to decouple market analysis from trade execution — for example, analyzing ES while routing trades to MES.

data.name: EnabledColorElementDescription
data.value: When enabled, displays the specified element and sets it to a specific color.

data.name: EnableTradeOnOneClickDescription
data.value: Activating One-Click Trading mode allows you to submit orders without additional confirmation. This speeds up the trading process but also increases the risk of errors and accidental actions. You should be aware that when enabling this mode, all responsibility for accidental clicks lies with you.

data.name: EndDayDescription
data.value: The end day of the trading platform

data.name: EndLineAsArrowDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display the end of the line as an arrow.

data.name: EndTimeDescription
data.value: The end time of the trading platform

data.name: EndTimeFilterDescription
data.value: Specifies the end time for the time filter.

data.name: EndTimeLessOrEqualToStartTime
data.value: End time less or equal to start time.

data.name: EnterDataProviderDetails
data.value: Please enter your trading account or data provider details and click the connect button

data.name: EqualizePositionsTooltip
data.value: Create trades to equalize positions with statistic

data.name: EquilibriumBottomCandleDescription
data.value: Determines the color of the element representing the lower wick of a doji candle with the same upper and lower wicks, which indicates balance between buyers and sellers.

data.name: EquilibriumTopCandleDescription
data.value: Determines the color of the element representing the upper wick of a doji candle with the same upper and lower wicks, which indicates balance between buyers and sellers.

data.name: ErgodicDescription
data.value: The indicator calculates price differences and their absolute values, applies exponential smoothing to them for short and long periods, and also calculates the true strength index (TSI) as the ratio of short-term exponential smoothing to long-term. The signal line (signal moving average) is calculated using a separate period, and the final value of the Ergodic indicator is formed as the difference between the TSI and the signal line. This process allows you to identify key points of change in market trends.

data.name: ErrorLoadingTariffsMessage
data.value:
Failed to load information about tariffs.
You can retry the download or close this window.

data.name: ErrorSaveWorkspaceOnClosingMessage
data.value: An error occurred while saving workspace.

data.name: EstimatorLineSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the estimator line.

data.name: ExhaustionIndDescription
data.value: The indicator detects a weakening of market activity when, as the price rises or falls, trading volumes gradually decrease. This indicates a decrease in demand, which is reflected in a decrease in volumes in the ASK (top of the candle) and BID (bottom of the candle) columns, as well as in the total volume.

data.name: ExitWizardPlaceholder
data.value: Share what you liked about this update and what could have been done better?

data.name: ExtendLinesRightDescription

data.value: Extend lines to start of next profile

data.name: ExtendLinesTillStartOfNextProfileDescription

data.name: ExtendOverSubChartsDescription
data.value: Determines whether to extend the line over sub-charts.

data.name: ExtendPreviousDescription

data.name: ExtendSelectedAreaDescription
data.value: Determines whether to extend the selected area.

data.name: ExternalChartDescription
data.value: Template for the External Chart indicator, which shows data from other timeframes. Supports unlimited instances to create multi-timeframe configurations. Clusters are displayed as a heatmap with auto-highlighting of key zones.

data.name: ExternalChartsDescription
data.value: The indicator allows “combining” several consecutive bars into bigger time intervals.

data.name: FadePeriodDescription
data.value: Defines the duration, in milliseconds, for the fading period of displayed items.

data.name: FailedStopReplayMessage
