data.name: FailedStoreDaysDescription
data.value: Specify how many days to store unused strategies

data.name: FailedToLoadPlatformSettingsMsg
data.value: Failed to load platform settings, using default settings.

data.name: FailedTryConnect
data.value: Connection error, please try again. If you cannot connect, please contact support.

data.name: FairValueGapDescription
data.value: The indicator finds fair value gaps on the current and higher timeframes. The fair value gap is determined on a three-bar range in which the leftmost high is lower than the rightmost low or the leftmost low is higher than the rightmost high.

data.name: FastConstDescription
data.value: Adjusts the sensitivity to short-term price fluctuations.

data.name: FastLineSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the fast line.

data.name: FavoriteGroupDescription
data.value:
Quick access to the indicators you use most often.

To add an indicator to Favorites, click the ⭐ next to its name.

  • Access your needed indicators in one click.
  • Focus on the tools you use in your work.
  • Save time searching through long lists.

data.name: FButtonTooltip
data.value: Autoscale is disabled. Click to enable.

data.name: FCVDescription
data.value: This indicator calculates and displays the full contract value with (or without) using a custom tick value.

data.name: FiboExtensionsObjDescription
data.value: This drawing object is an advanced version of Fibo Retracement, with the difference that it allows you to identify potential target levels for further price movement after the rollback is complete.

data.name: FiboFansObjDescription
data.value: This drawing object is designed to visualize Fibonacci levels using radial lines emanating from a given point on the chart, creating a fan effect.

data.name: FiboLinesDescription
data.value: Defines the set of lines used in the Fibo fan.

data.name: FiboLinesSettingsDescription
data.value: Defines the settings for the Fibo lines.

data.name: FiboPercentageDescription
data.value: Determines the value of the Fibo level.

data.name: FiboRetracementObjDescription
data.value: This drawing object is designed to visualize Fibonacci retracement levels on a selected chart area. This tool is used by traders to identify potential support and resistance levels that may occur after a significant price move.

data.name: FiboReverseDescription
data.value: Determines whether to reverse the Fibo sequence.

data.name: FieldsRangeErrorFormat
data.value: {0}' must be in the range from {1} to {2}

data.name: FillAreaDescription
data.value: Determines whether to paint the specified area with the specified color.

data.name: FillColorDescription
data.value: Sets the fill color of an element.

data.name: FillLastPrice
data.value: Fill in the current last price.

data.name: FilterCandleColorDescription
data.value: Sets the color for candles that match the filter criteria.

data.name: FilterColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color for the element that does not pass the filter.

data.name: FilterPassColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color of the element that passed the filter.

data.name: FiltersDescription
data.value: Relative modes are controlled by 'Proportion settings' section

data.name: FiltersMaxVolumeDecimalColorDescription
data.value: Defines a list of filters with a given maximum volume and corresponding color.

data.name: FindCredentialText
data.value: You can find your credentials in the

data.name: FinishInterviewText1
data.value: You always can add a new connection of your account for online data and trading. This can be done in the Home -> Connectors section.

data.name: FinishInterviewText2
data.value: Take a look at our template gallery! Here you will find ready-to-use charts, bid/ask tapes, DOMs... Each template has a description of its purpose and ultimate benefit.

data.name: FinishRateText
data.value: Thank you very much for your feedback!

data.name: FirstBarDescription
data.value: If enabled, the display of the element begins with the bar that participated in the calculations.

data.name: FirstNameMustBeLatinError
data.value: First name must be in Latin alphabet

data.name: FisherTransformDescription
data.value: The Fisher Transform is a technical indicator that converts prices into a Gaussian normal distribution. In this way, the indicator highlights when prices have moved to an extreme, based on recent prices. This may help in spotting turning points in the price of an asset. It also helps show the trend and isolate the price waves within a trend.

data.name: FisherTransformInverseDescription
data.value: This indicator is inverse to Fisher Transform. This conversion function outputs values from -1 to 1, which makes it suitable for using as an oscillator.

data.name: FisherTransformInverseRsiDescription
data.value: Inverse Fisher Transform with RSI is a variation of the Inverse Fisher Transform oscillator, where the RSI indicator values are taken as intermediate values.

data.name: FitToPriceRangeDescription
data.value: Draw session area only between the high and low of the period

data.name: FixedSizesDescription
data.value: When this option is turned on, all volumes will be displayed with a similar size, regardless of their actual volume. This can be useful for standardizing the visual representation of different volumes on the chart.

data.name: FlattenAllConfirmationMessage
data.value: Are you sure you want to close/cancel all positions and orders? This action will affect all selected instruments and cannot be undone

data.name: FOKDescription
data.value: FOK (Fill Or Kill): Order must be filled immediately in its entirety or not executed at all.

data.name: FontSettingDescription
data.value: Specifies various settings for the font.

data.name: ForceIndexDescription
data.value: The Force Index indicator - is calculated as the difference between the current closing price and the previous one, multiplied by the trading volume in the current candle. The main idea of the indicator is to determine the force of pressure on the quotes of buyers and sellers.

data.name: ForexDescription
data.value: The ATAS platform does not support Forex pairs, because for forex pairs it is impossible to get real trading volumes. However, forex pairs are highly correlated with currency futures traded on CME.

data.name: FractalPeriodDescription
data.value: The number of bars on each side of the center bar required to identify a fractal.

data.name: FractalsDescription
data.value: The indicator identifies and presents local extremes based on a 5-candle pattern. A high extremum occurs when the highest point is at the center candle, flanked by lower highs on both sides. Conversely, a low extremum is characterized by the lowest point at the center candle, surrounded by higher lows on both sides.

data.name: FromDateGreaterToDataMessage
data.value: You cannot specify a "From" date that is later than a "To" date.

data.name: FullLinesDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display full lines.

data.name: FundingRateColumnDescription
data.value:
Funding fee is exchanged directly between buyers and sellers every 8 hours. When the funding rate is positive, long position holders pay to short position holders. When the funding rate is negative, short position holders pay long position holders.
Funding fee = position value * funding rate

data.name: GapsIndDescription
data.value: The indicator allows you to determine price gaps between neighboring bars.

data.name: GlobalAlert
data.value: Receive alerts when chart is closed

data.name: GlobalAlertDescription
data.value: Enables receiving alerts even if the instrument has been switched

data.name: GMMADescription
data.value: The Guppy Multiple Moving Average (GMMA) indicator is a trending technical indicator that uses 6 short-term and 6 long-term moving averages to determine the strength and duration of a trend.

data.name: GradientColorDescription
data.value: Specifies the alternate color to use for the gradient.

data.name: GradientDescription
data.value: Works if 'Visualization Mode' set to 'Bars' mode

data.name: Great
data.value: Great, we can get to work!

data.name: GreatestSwingDescription
data.value: The indicator is based on the GSV concept from the book "Long-Term Secrets of Short-Term Trading" by Larry Williams. Unlike Larry Williams' concept, the calculations involve not the high and low of the day, but the extremes of a particular bar. The indicator represents a channel within which the price can move.

data.name: GridColorDescription
data.value: Visual data separation for quick information reading.

data.name: GridLineSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the grid line.

data.name: GridRowHeihgtDescription
data.value: Sets the height of the grid row.

data.name: GTCDescription
data.value: GTC (Good Till Cancel): Order will continue to work until filled or canceled.

data.name: HasActiveCopyOrdersForProtectiveOrdersMessage
data.value: Account {0} is signed for copying orders; when opening protective orders, a position reversal may occur

data.name: HasActiveCopyPositionssForProtectiveOrdersMessage
data.value: Account {0} is subscribed to copy positions; when protective orders are opened, a position reversal may occur

data.name: HasActiveStrategiesForCopyOrdersToFollowingPortfoliosMessage
data.value:
Accounts signed up for copying orders have active protective strategies - this can lead to unpredictable consequences when trading.
Start copying orders?

data.name: HasActiveStrategiesForCopyPositionsToFollowingPortfoliosMessage
data.value:
Accounts signed up for copying positions have active defensive strategies - this can lead to unpredictable consequences when trading.
Start copying positions?

data.name: HasChangedIndicators
data.value: Some indicator libraries have been changed. You can reload indicators in the status bar of the main window.

data.name: HasChangedStrategies
data.value: Some strategy libraries have been changed. You can reload strategies in the status bar of the main window.

data.name: HasHiddenSettingsFilesMessage
data.value: Hidden and/or read-only files were found in the platform settings folder {0}. This can disrupt the platform functioning. To continue, all hidden files must be visible. Fix file attributes and continue?

data.name: HasInvalidCharactersForNameError
data.value: The name must not contain the following characters: {0}.

data.name: HasNotFixedProblemsMessage
data.value: A number of issues have been successfully fixed. However, there are modules which require manual intervention. They are highlighted with the corresponding sign.

data.name: HasOnlyManuallyDisableSLTPText
data.value: There are SL/TP orders that can only be disabled manually

data.name: HasSkipLoadingPanelsFromConnectors
data.value: There are unloaded modules, to load modules, connect the following connectors: {0}.

data.name: HasUserValuesMessage
data.value: Some values have been changed by the user

data.name: HeaderBackgroundDescription
data.value: Header highlighting for quick row navigation.

data.name: HeatmapSwitchToOpenGLRender
data.value: To display the volume map, change the rendering settings to a supported mode (OpenGL).

data.name: HeatmapTemplateDescription
data.value: Anticipate price movements with the liquidity map — a chart showing where limit orders are placed. See the levels the market "feels" and use them for precise entries and exits.

data.name: HeatmapTypeDesctiption
data.value: Works if 'Custom color scheme' is disabled

data.name: HedgingModeDescription
data.value: Hedging mode enables you to use the same account to copy positions across multiple groups at the same time, with each group having its own separate position accounting. This feature is used for copying positions from multiple accounts to one or more follower accounts.

data.name: HeikenAshiDescription
data.value: Heiken Ashi (HA) - a type of representation of quotations on time segments based on the averaged price fluctuations of the previous time period.

data.name: HeikenAshiSmoothedDescription
data.value: Heiken Ashi Smoothed is based on the Heiken Ashi indicator. The indicator values are calculated similarly to the original, and smoothing by weighted and smoothed moving averages is applied additionally to them. Smoothing is applied to all the original indicator values by the formula: WMA(SMMA(x(t))).

data.name: HerrickPayoffDescription
data.value: Herrick Payoff Index (HPI) is an index, which shows inflow or outflow of funds from the futures market. The formula was developed by John Herrick and was based on the analysis of price, open interest and trading volume changes.

data.name: HideBarsCountUnfilteredRangesDescription
data.value: Determines whether to hide ranges that do not pass the bars count filter.

data.name: HideBorderDescription
data.value: Determines whether to hide the display of the specified element's borders.

data.name: HideGapsDescription
data.value: Determines whether to hide the display of gaps on the chart.

data.name: HideLabelBackGroundDescription
data.value: This option hides the display of the label background.

data.name: HideMaxVolumeUnfilteredRangesDescription
data.value: Determines whether to hide ranges that do not pass the maximum volume filter.

data.name: HideOldsElementsDescription
data.value: Allows you to hide these elements on the history, displaying only the current ones.

data.name: HidePriceLabelDescription
data.value: Determines whether to hide the label on the price scale.

data.name: HideTextDescription
data.value: Determines whether to hide the given text.

data.name: HighestIndDescription
data.value: The indicator shows the maximum price for a number of bars specified in the period.

data.name: HighestLowestPeriodDescription
data.value: Sets the period of the highest and lowest components.

data.name: HighlightCloses
data.value: Highlight close of each sub period

data.name: HighlightColorDescription
data.value: Specifies the highlight color for the specified element.

data.name: HighlightOpens
data.value: Highlight open of each new sub period

data.name: HighlightSignalBarsDescription
data.value: Determines whether to highlight signal bars.

data.name: HighlightXObjDescription
data.value: Highlight X is a tool that highlights an area on the chart limited by the specified left and right boundaries, as well as the top and bottom edges of the chart.

data.name: HighlightYObjDescription
data.value: Highlight Y is a tool that highlights the area of a chart limited by the specified top and bottom boundaries, as well as the left and right edges of the chart.

data.name: HighLowIndDescription
data.value: This indicator displays the maximum and minimum values for last n bars on the chart.

data.name: HighLowLineColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color to display the high/low line.

data.name: HighRatioColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color for high ratio elements.

data.name: HighRatioDescription
data.value: Set the value of the high boundary for ratio.

data.name: HistogramMinimizedModeDescription
data.value: In this mode, all histogram elements are displayed above the zero line.

data.name: HistogramWidthDescription
data.value: Defines the width of the histogram element.

data.name: HLCAveragePrevSessionDescription
data.value: The average price value for the previous session, calculated using the formula (high + low + close) / 3.

data.name: HLineObjDescription
data.value: A horizontal line is a tool that displays a line running from the left to the right edge of the chart at a given price level.

data.name: HMADescription
data.value: The Hull Moving Average (HMA) is a widely used method for calculating averages in pricing. It was created by Alan Hull in 2005 to overcome the limitations of conventional moving averages, particularly issues like lag and noise. The HMA tackles these challenges by using weighted moving averages, resulting in smoother price data with reduced noise.

data.name: HotKeyAlreadyUsedFormat
data.value: Keyboard shortcut {0} is already used in: {1}. Change keyboard shortcut?

data.name: HRangesDescription
data.value: The HRanges indicator is designed to determine and display price ranges based on certain conditions. Local extrema consisting of 5 candles are considered. At the time of formation, the range is considered flat. After the breakout, depending on which direction the breakout was in, this range is considered bullish or bearish. It is possible to set filters by maximum volume and number of bars in the range.

data.name: HRayObjDescription
data.value: A horizontal ray is a tool that displays a line at a given level, starting at a specific location on the chart and ending at the price scale.

data.name: HurstExponentDescription
data.value: The Hurst Exponent serves as an indicator for assessing the long-term memory within a time series. It reflects the autocorrelation of time series data and how quickly this correlation diminishes with increasing time lags between pairs of values. The Hurst Exponent is often viewed as a "dependence index" or a "long-term dependence index." It quantifies the tendency of a time series to either consistently decrease in the average value direction or to concentrate in a particular direction relative to its past behavior.

data.name: HVRDescription
data.value: The indicator Historical Volatility Ratio (HVR) is designed to assess the historical volatility of an instrument. It calculates the ratio between short-term and long-term volatility based on the logarithmic returns of closing prices. A rising ratio might indicate increasing short-term volatility relative to long-term one, suggesting a possible change in trend dynamics.

data.name: IBPreviousDayEquityWithLoanValue
data.value: Previous Day Equity With Loan Value

data.name: IchimokuDescription
data.value: Ichimoku Kinko Hyo - a technical analysis tool that allows you to identify support and resistance, determine the trend, measure momentum and create trading signals.

data.name: IgnoredByAlertsDescription
data.value: Specifies whether the value of this data series should be ignored by the alerts set for drawing objects.

data.name: IgnoreWicksDescription
data.value: Data for calculations is taken between the opening and closing of candles, if the condition is active, otherwise - between high and low.

data.name: IgnoreZeroValuesDescription
data.value: If enabled, zero values are not included in the calculations.

data.name: ImageLocationDescription
data.value: Specifies the display location of the image.

data.name: ImageOffsetXDescription
data.value: Sets the x-axis offset of the image in pixels.

data.name: ImageOffsetYDescription
data.value: Sets the y-axis offset of the image in pixels.

data.name: ImageTransparencyDescription
data.value: Defines a value for the transparency component of an image.

data.name: ImbalanceRatioIndDescription
data.value: The indicator exhibits imbalances between bid and ask orders based on a specified ratio, along with the count of such imbalances within a given bar.

data.name: Import workspace from previous version now?"
ImportLongLoadWorkspaceMessage
data.value: Opening workspace can create a serious load on a PC due to a large number of opened modules. We advise you to edit it before opening. Besides, you can just open this workspace or import without opening. Please, select an action.

data.name: ImportOldStatisticsError
data.value: An error occurred while importing past statistics. To fix the problem, contact technical support.

data.name: ImportOldWorkspaceHint
data.value: Import workspace file from previous version

data.name: ImportOldWorkspaceMessage
data.value: You can import workspaces from the previous version using the "Import" button in the toolbar of the main window in the "Settings" tab. Import workspace from previous version now?

data.name: ImportSettingsMessage
data.value: ATAS settings found. Do you want to import them?

data.name: ImpossibleChangeMarginTypeFtx
data.value: FTX does not support isolated margin mode. Use subaccounts instead

data.name: ImpossibleChangeMarginTypeMessage
data.value: The margin mode cannot be changed while you have an open order/position.

data.name: ImpossibleToReduceLeverage
data.value: Leverage reduction is not supported for open positions in isolated margin mode

data.name: ImprovedCommentLabel
data.value: What needs to be done better?

data.name: IncludeEqualsValuesDescription
data.value: Determines whether to take into account cases where the compared values are equal.

data.name: IncreasedMomentumSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the element representing the increased Momentum value.

data.name: IndicatorCurrentVersionDescription
data.value: Defines the current version number of the indicator.

data.name: IndicatorLoadError
data.value: Failed to load indicator settings. The indicator may be incompatible with this version of the platform.

data.name: IndicatorProtectedFromDebugging
data.value: The indicator is protected from debugging.

data.name: InertiaIndDescription
data.value: Inertia - the indicator is an alternative to moving averages, which calculates the expected price movement while maintaining the current balance of buyers and sellers. The Linear Regression and Relative Vigor Index indicators are involved in the calculations.

data.name: InertiaV2IndDescription
data.value: Inertia V2 is an alternative to moving averages, which calculates the expected price movement while maintaining the current balance of buyers and sellers. The Linear Regression and Relative Vigor Index indicators are involved in the calculations. In contrast to the first version of the indicator, the Inertia V2 uses a more complex formula and also uses the Standard Deviation indicator.

data.name: InitialBalanceIndDescription
data.value: Initial Balance (starting balance) - indicator, which selects the starting area from "High" to "Low" of a specified period, and builds lines on the chart in accordance with initial values. It is believed that if the starting balance is narrow compared to the previous day, you can expect a breakthrough price for this zone. If the starting balance is wide, the price is likely to be hovering just inside the area.

data.name: InitialBalanceRangeDescription
data.value: Determines the number of sub-periods included in the initial balance.

data.name: InitialBalanceWidthDescription
data.value: Defines the width of the initial balance.

data.name: InsideEqualsBarDescription
data.value: Inside Bar indicator highlights series of bars which bodies are within the range between the high and low of the first bar in each highlighted series. The Tolerance parameters allow you to set an acceptable tolerance of the candle's body from the high/low.

data.name: InstrumentDontSupportTimeFrame
data.value: The selected instrument does not support.

data.name: InstrumentLicenseExpired
data.value: Your license for this instrument is expired.

data.name: InstrumentNotAvailableInReplayMode
data.value: This instrument is not available in Replay mode, please choose another instrument.

data.name: InstrumentNotLoadedStrategySettingsHint
data.value: The instrument is not loaded. Open a chart or Smart DOM for this instrument to enable strategy settings.

data.name: InstrumentNotSupportedByLiquidationsFeed
data.value: Current instrument is not supported by the Liquidations data feed.

data.name: InstrumentNotSupportedByLongShortRatioFeed
data.value: Current instrument is not supported by the Long/Short Ratio data feed.

data.name: InstrumentNotSupportedByOpenInterestFeed
data.value: Current instrument is not supported by the Open Interest data feed.

data.name: InstrumentsSettingsResetedMessage
data.value: Failed to load tool settings, so favorite tools were loaded by default.

data.name: InsufficientDataForPeriod
data.value: There is insufficient data for this period.

data.name: InterestAnalysisQuestion
data.value: What analytical instruments are you most interested in?

data.name: InterestMarketQuestion
data.value: What markets are you most interested in?

data.name: InvalidWebhookUrl
data.value: Please enter a valid webhook link.

data.name: InvalidWorkspaceForLoad
data.value: The workspace is damaged and cannot be loaded.

data.name: InvertOutputDescription
data.value: Determines whether it is necessary to invert (change the sign of the value to the opposite) the output values.

data.name: IOCDescription
data.value: IOC (Immediate Or Cancel): Order will execute all or partial immediately and cancel any unfilled portion of the order.

data.name: IsApproximationAlertDescription
data.value: Determines whether an alert is issued when the price approaches a certain level.

data.name: IsCustomSessionDescription
data.value: If enabled, allows the use of a custom session, otherwise the default session.

data.name: IsLeftToRightDirectionDescription
data.value: If enabled, displays the specified element from left to right, otherwise from right to left.

data.name: IsLimitMaxLengthDescription
data.value: Determines whether to limit the maximum length of the specified element.

data.name: IsLineTillTouchDescription
data.value: Determines whether to extend the line until it touches the bar.

data.name: IsNeedFillDescription
data.value: Determines whether the displayed element should be filled.

data.name: IsNeedShowCurrentTimeDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display the current time.

data.name: IsNeedShowLabelDescription
data.value: Determines whether the text label should be displayed.

data.name: IsNeedShowLinesDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display lines on the chart.

data.name: IsolatedMarginModeDescription
data.value: Manage your risk on individual positions by restricting the amount of margin allocated to each. If the margin ratio of a position reached 100%, the position will be liquidated. Margin can be added or removed to positions using this mode.

data.name: IsSingalAlertDescription
data.value: Determines whether to issue an alert when a specified signal appears.

data.name: IsVisibleDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display this element.

data.name: KAMADescription
data.value: Indicator Kaufman Adaptive Moving Average (KAMA) - is a type of adaptive moving average based on an exponentially smoothed moving average and an original technique for determining and applying volatility as a dynamically changing smoothing constant.

data.name: KdFastDescription
data.value: Indicator KD-Fast, also known as Fast Stochastic, is a type of stochastic oscillator developed by George C. Lane. It evaluates the relationship between the current closing price and the range of highs and lows over a certain period. Essentially, it calculates the rate of price change. The KD-Fast indicator is represented on the chart by two lines: %K and %D. These lines provide insight into momentum and potential trend reversal points.

data.name: KDJDescription
data.value: The KDJ indicator is a combination of 3 lines: fast, medium and slow, crossing of which gives a signal to open a trade. A buy signal is formed when the lines are crossed: the red line is above the blue one, and the blue line above the green one. A sell signal is received when crossing in the opposite order: the red line is below the blue one, the blue line is below the green one. Note that the farther apart the lines are, the stronger the signal. The calculation is based on the KD-Slow indicator.

data.name: KdSlowDescription
data.value: Indicator KD - Slow is a changed KD - Fast indicator. Just like in the KD - Fast, two lines are drawn, only the %K line is a %D line of the KD - Fast indicator, and the %D is a smoothed %K line.

data.name: KeltnerChannelDescription
data.value: Indicator Keltner Channel (MetaTrader indicator) is a classic technical analysis indicator developed by Chester Keltner in 1960. It uses three chart lines: the medium line is the ten-day simple moving average for “typical” ((high + low + close) / 3) prices, the upper and lower lines are obtained by adding and subtracting the moving average of the daily price range (difference between the maximum and minimum) from the medium line.

data.name: KurtosisDescription
data.value: The Kurtosis indicator is a market mood indicator. It consists of three parts: Kurtosis, Fast Kurtosis and Fast/Slow Kurtosis. The indicator itself is shown on the chart by two curves: Fast Kurtosis and Fast/Slow Kurtosis. The indicator shows the normal distribution dynamics on the chart. Positive values indicate the normal distribution peak values, while negative values indicate the opposite.

data.name: LabelDisplayDescription
data.value: Control label visibility: Hide, Short (direction + result), Full (with prices)

data.name: LabelLocationDescription
data.value: Specifies the display location of the label.

data.name: LabelObjDescription
data.value: This drawing object allows you to display a text label on the chart window. The label is bound to the window coordinates.

data.name: LabelOffsetXDescription
data.value: Sets the x-axis offset of the label in pixels.

data.name: LabelOffsetYDescription
data.value: Sets the y-axis offset of the label in pixels.

data.name: LabelTextColorDescription
data.value: Sets the color of the label text.

data.name: LabelTextDescription
data.value: Defines the text in the displayed label.

data.name: LabelWidthDescription
data.value: Defines the width of the label.

data.name: LaggingLinePeriodDescription
data.value: The period measured in bars for the lagging line.

data.name: LaggingLineSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the lagging line.

data.name: LargeChangeScaleDescription
data.value: Key-Modifier on mouse scroll to change price scale by 10x

data.name: LargeTradersLineSettingsDescription
data.value: Line visualization settings reflect the positions of major players.

data.name: LastNameMustBeLatinError
data.value: Last name must be in Latin alphabet

data.name: LastReportTimeDescription
data.value: This field indicates the date of the last report.

data.name: LatestMessage
data.value: The latest version is a release candidate, which will appear one or two months before a new stable platform version release. This variant has updated functionality and fixes, which passed beta-testing and are at the final stage of revision.

data.name: LatestVersionDescription
data.value: Users’ choice. Released more often than Stable, but with fewer issues than Beta

data.name: LeftPriceMetkaObjDescription
data.value: This drawing object is designed to display price labels on a chart, where the price value is located to the left of the label itself.

data.name: LevelsDescription
data.value: Number of order book levels to analyze. More levels provide a more complete liquidity picture.

data.name: LevelValidTimeFilterDescription
data.value: If enabled, determines the time at which the found level is considered valid.

data.name: LeverageLimitDescription
data.value: Allows to limit marginal leverage for convenience. Use 0 to disable limit.

data.name: LicenseNotSupportTrading
data.value: Your license does not support trading at this account. Please contact support.

data.name: LimitDescription
data.value: A limit order is an order to buy or sell at a specific price or better. Limit orders are not guaranteed to execute.

data.name: LimitedConnectionMessage
data.value: Your account is expired, you have a free access to crypto exchanges and restricted access for other exchanges. To continue having a full-featured access to the platform you have to subscribe a paid service.

data.name: LimitTrackingDescriptin
data.value: Advanced template for tracking changes in limit orders. Highlights large orders and volume changes using filters. Useful for analyzing institutional actions.

data.name: LinearRegDescription
data.value: This indicator allows trading using the so called channel strategy, which is based on the support and resistance levels. Linear Regression allows simplifying the buildup of a channel required for trade. It enables not to set individually significant points as the program builds up the channel itself, based on the set time frame.

data.name: LinearRegIndDescription
data.value: The indicator Linear Regression calculates and displays the linear regression of a financial instrument's price over a specified period. It represents the trend by fitting a straight line to historical data, showing the direction and strength of the trend. Traders can adjust the period to control sensitivity. The indicator helps identify trends, potential turning points, and provides an approximation of overall price movement based on linear regression analysis. However, it's essential to use it in conjunction with other tools for comprehensive market analysis.

data.name: LinearRegPeriodDescription
data.value: Sets the period for the "Linear Regression".

data.name: LineColorDescription
data.value: Determines the color of the displayed line.

data.name: LineDashStyleDescription
data.value: Specifies the dash pattern used for lines in the chart, allowing selection of styles like solid, dotted, dashed, or custom patterns for visual differentiation

data.name: LineLengthDescription
data.value: Determines the length of the displayed line.

data.name: LineSettingsDescription
data.value: This setting allows you to specify the line display parameters.

data.name: LineValueDescription
data.value: Defines the numeric level corresponding to this line.

data.name: LineWidthDescription
data.value: Determines the width of the displayed line.

data.name: LinRegChannelDescription
data.value: The indicator displays a channel on the price chart with a width equal to the specified deviation, using Linear Regression Slope. This indicator is used to determine the direction and strength of a trend.

data.name: LinRegSlopeDescription
data.value: The Linear Regression Slope indicator shows the slope component of the Linear Regression line. The indicator is designed to display the time required to change the price per specified period.

data.name: LiquidationOrdersAggregationModeGlobalDescription
data.value: Aggregates all available liquidation orders across symbols and exchanges.

data.name: LiquidationOrdersAggregationModeLocalDescription
data.value: Aggregates liquidation orders for a specific symbol on a specific exchange.

data.name: LiquidationOrdersAggregationModesDescription
data.value: Defines how liquidation events are aggregated.

data.name: LiquidationOrdersAggregationModeSymbolGlobalDescription

data.name: LowerDeviationSettingsDescription

data.name: LowerWickSettingsDescription

data.name: LowestIndDescription
data.value: The indicator shows the minimum price for a certain number of bars in a period, taking into account the selected source.

data.name: LowLevelTransparencyDescription
data.value: Determines whether to use transparency for elements with small values.

data.name: LowRatioColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color for low ratio elements.

data.name: LowRatioDescription
data.value: Set the value of the low boundary for ratio.

data.name: MacdBbImprovedDescription
data.value: It is an improved version of the MACD Bollinger Bands indicator. In this indicator, Bollinger Bands values have a lower lag than in the original one.

data.name: MacdBbStandartDescription
data.value: It is an enhanced version of the MACD indicator. In addition to the MACD, the BollingerBands indicator, which is calculated from the MACD values, is added to the chart.

data.name: MACDDescription
data.value: The MACD (moving average convergence divergence) indicator refers, on the one hand, to the oscillator* class and, on the other hand, to the complex averages class because it is nothing but only the improved visual perception of two simple moving averages (or exponential moving averages). There are two ways to build and analyze MACD: - MACD linear, which is more frequently used for trend analysis - MACD histogram, which rather refers to the oscillator class by the analysis method and value. *This indicator in the ATAS platform has been implemented using the combined moving average and histogram.

data.name: MacdLeaderDescription
data.value: The indicator plots the standard MACD indicator with the additional Leader line. Smoothing methods have lag, and since the MACD uses moving averages, it usually lags behind the price. It is impossible to eliminate the lag completely, but there is one workaround: to add the price/moving average difference component back to the moving average. This technique is called Zero-lag. The Leader MACD uses it to generate a leading signal for the MACD.

data.name: MACDVsSignalDiffSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the difference between MACD line and signal line.

data.name: MacdVWDescription
data.value: This indicator (VW-MACD) was created by Buff Dormeier and described in his book “Investing With Volume Analysis”. It represents the convergence and divergence of volume weighted average price trends. The use of volume makes the VM-MACD more sensitive and reliable than the traditional MACD. When calculating the MACD difference, the Volume Weighted MACD replaces two exponential moving averages with two corresponding volume weighted moving averages. Thus, the VW-MACD separates a volume weighted short-term trend from a volume weighted long-term trend. The signal line remains as an exponential moving average because the VW-MACD line is already volume weighted.

data.name: MaDifferenceDescription
data.value: Moving Average Difference calculates the difference between two simple moving averages with different periods. It is calculated by the following formula: ΔSMA = SMA1(x(t)) - SMA2(x(t)).

data.name: MaEnvelopeDescription
data.value: Moving Average Envelope is the percentage value set above and below the moving average. In this case, each Envelope is set to a percentage above or below the moving average. This creates parallel bands that follow the price action. If the moving average is used as a base, the Moving Average Envelope can be used as a trend-following indicator. However, besides just following the trend, Envelope can also be used to determine overbought and oversold levels when the trend is not defined.

data.name: MaintenanceMarginColumnDescription
data.value: The minimum amount of margin balance required to keep your open positions.

data.name: MaintenanceText
data.value: Server is currently undergoing maintenance and upgrades by our technical support team. We apologize for any inconvenience.

data.name: MarginColumnDescription
data.value: The position margin includes the initial margin and closing fee. You may append extra margin or adjust the leverage applied to change the position margin.

data.name: MarginCrossDescription
data.value: Under cross margin, all available balance of the corresponding margin account will be deployed to meet maintenance margin requirements and prevent liquidation. All corresponding available balance can be lost in the event of liquidation. Please note that adjusting the leverage will affect all positions and active orders under the current pair.

data.name: MarginIsolatedDescription
data.value: Under isolated margin, a specific amount of margin, i.e. initial margin, is applied to a position, and position margin can be adjusted manually. In the event of a liquidation, you may lose the initial margin and extra margin added to this position. Please note that adjusting the leverage will affect all positions and active orders under the current pair.

data.name: MarginRateColumnDescription
data.value: The lower the Margin Ratio, the lower your liquidation level will be relative to your position size.

data.name: MarginTypeDescription
data.value:
Cross Margin Mode: All cross positions under the same margin asset share the same asset cross margin balance. In the event of liquidation, your assets full margin balance along with any remaining open positions under the asset may be forfeited.

Isolated Margin Mode: Manage your risk on individual positions by restricting the amount of margin allocated to each. If the margin ratio of a position reached 100%, the position will be liquidated. Margin can be added or removed to positions using this mode.

data.name: MarginValueDescription
data.value: Margin values for the instrument can be viewed on the exchange.

data.name: MarginZonesDescription
data.value:
This indicator allows you to automatically calculate and visualize margin zones to the specified parameters. Margin Zone Level = Margin / Price Tick.
Calculation: Margin Zone Level = Margin / Price Tick. For example, take the euro futures, the ticker 6E. Margin = $ 2300 Tick price = $ 6.25 Marginal zone level = 2300 / 6.25 = 368 ticks. This means that the margin zone will be built at a distance of 368 ticks from the initial price. The height of the marginal zone is 10% of this distance, that is, 36 ticks.

data.name: MarketAndVolumeDescription
data.value:
This book is a practical guide to financial market analysis. We will focus on the concepts of Market Profile and Volume Profile, two important instruments that traders around the world use for conducting effective analysis and making informed trading decisions.
Let's start by diving into the market auction theory. Let us analyze the most significant concepts: balance, imbalance, value area, etc. These concepts are the basis for understanding the more complex aspects of the market, which we will discuss next.
In the second part of the book, we will move on to a thorough study of the Market Profile. We will examine in detail how Time Price Opportunity (TPO) charts are created, how to interpret them correctly, and what main patterns can be identified.
Having mastered Market Profile, we will proceed to studying Volume Profile - a more advanced and sophisticate instrument of market analysis. Just like with Market Profile, we will take a detailed look at the key concepts, patterns, and strategies for working with Volume Profile.
In addition, you will find a detailed guide on setting up and using various ATAS modules closely related to Market Profile and Volume Profile. This will allow you to effectively use all the platform's features for your trading needs.

data.name: MarketDataTabConnectedStateTextFormat
data.value: Right now, you have access to market data, allowing you to monitor {0} instruments without a real account connection. To switch to live trading, open the Account tab and link your account.

data.name: MarketDataTabDisconnectedStateText

data.name: MarketDepthHeatmapDescription
data.value: Combine price and liquidity in one window. This template overlays limit order data directly on the candlestick chart for faster analysis and decision-making.

data.name: MarketDescription
data.value: Market order is immediately matched to the best available market price.

data.name: MarketFacilitationDescription
data.value: The MFI (Market Facilitation Index) indicator measures how the market reacts to changes in volume by analyzing the relationship between price changes and volume changes over a period of time. MFI = (High – Low) / Volume.

data.name: MarketOrdersDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display buttons for market orders.

data.name: MarketPowerDescription

data.name: MarketProfiliesTPODescription
data.value: An indicator that allows you to build periodic market profiles. Also contains a classic TPO profile and initial balance.

data.name: MarkPriceColumnDescription
data.value: Latest mark price for this contract. To prevent price manipulation, the mark price is used for unrealized PNL and margin calculations, and may differ from the last price. Mark Price is calculated based on the index price. The Price Index is a bucket of prices from the major Spot market exchanges, weighted by their relative volume.

The average trade is calculated by dividing the volume of a price level by the number of ticks on that level. If the resulting value is greater than this property, the price level will be excluded from consideration.

data.name: McClellanOscillatorDescription
data.value: The McClellan Oscillator is a technical market indicator, which characterizes the market width through analysis of a number of growing and falling instruments in the period and is one of the most widely used indicators of this type.

data.name: MeanDevDescription
data.value: The Mean Deviation indicator is a technical analysis tool that calculates the mean deviation of the price from its simple moving average (SMA) over a specified period.

data.name: MeasureObjDescription

data.name: MFIDescription
data.value: This indicator shows the intensity with which money is invested or withdrawn from security. In other words, MFI is a tool to measure the speed of price movement based on the volume of trades. To do this, positive cash flows and negative cash flows and their ratios are compared.

If the resulting value is less than this property, the price level will be excluded from consideration.

data.name: MMedDescription
data.value: This indicator is similar to the simple moving average, except that the final result is not an arithmetic mean of the values taken, but their median.

data.name: MomentumDescription
data.value: This indicator shows the price change rate and is one of the simplest and most efficient technical analysis tools.

data.name: MomentumOscillatorDescription
data.value: The Price Momentum Oscillator (PMO), created by Carl Swenlin, is a rate-of-price oscillator that uses double smoothing through exponential moving averages. It oscillates around the zero line, where its direction indicates whether the force is strengthening or weakening. The steepness of the trend angle reflects the strength of the price movement.

data.name: MomentumTrendDescription
data.value: The Momentum Trend indicator is based on the Momentum indicator. It displays whether the Momentum value increased or decreased relative to the previous bar.

data.name: MoveSopLossToBreakevenProfitOnPostionMustTisksMessage
data.value: To move Stop-Loss to breakeven, the profit on the position must be more than {0} tick.

data.name: MoveStopLossToBreakevenHasPreviousErrorsMessage
data.value: To move Stop-Loss to breakeven, it is necessary to fix the previous SL/TP errors.

data.name: MoveStopLossToBreakevenMustOpenPositionMessage
data.value: To move Stop-Loss to breakeven, there must be an open position.

data.name: MoveStopLossToBreakevenOnlySupportedSimpleSLTPMessage
data.value: Move Stop-Loss to breakeven is supported only for Simple SL/TP mode.

data.name: MovingAverageIndDescription
data.value: The indicator combines the most popular moving average indicators. The user only needs to select the desired indicator type.

data.name: MovingTypeDescription
data.value: Defines the type of moving average.

data.name: MSIDescription
data.value: McClellan Summation Index is a cumulative sum of the McClellan oscillator. It is thought that the McClellan summation index also gives trading signals when moving into the area of positive/negative values: moving into an area above zero is a bullish signal, while moving into an area below zero is a bearish signal.

data.name: MultiMarketPowerDescription
data.value: This indicator allows displaying a few Market Power indicators in one window simultaneously.

data.name: Multiplier1Description

data.name: Multiplier2Description

data.name: MultiplierDescription

data.name: MurreyMathDescription
data.value: Murrey Math is a trading approach crafted by Prof. TH Murrey, rooted in the principles of WD Gann. The indicator encompasses various levels, ranging from 0/8 to 8/8. Each level has distinct operational characteristics; certain levels function more effectively as support and resistance, while others act as attractors for the price.

data.name: MutualFundBarsDescription
data.value: The indicator Mutual Fund Bars uses a candlestick format in which the close of the current candle and the close of the previous one are used for the OHLC data and the direction of the candle.

data.name: ObjectSizeDescription
data.value: Sets the size of the displayed objects.

data.name: OBVDescription
data.value: Technical indicator of the balance volume (On Balance Volume, OBV) binds volume to price change, which accompanied this voume. The meaning of this indicator is simple. If the closing price of the current bar is higher than closing price of previous, value of the volume of the current bar is added to the previous value OBV, if closing price of the current bar is lower than the previous, the current volume is subtracted from the previous value of the OBV.

data.name: OCOGroupDescription
data.value: OCO (One Cancels the Other) order allows you to place two orders at the same time. It combines a limit order, with a stop-limit order, but only one of the two can be executed.

data.name: OcoOrdersAgreement
data.value: OCO (one cancels other) orders for {0} are locally simulated on your PC. This means that {1} must be connected to your broker in order for the OCO capability to function. If you are disconnected and one of the OCO orders are filled, the other WILL NOT be cancelled.

data.name: OHLCPlusDescription
data.value: Extended price levels indicator showing OHLC, Equilibrium, POC and Value Area levels for

data.name: OIAnalyzerCumulativeTradesModeDescription
data.value: Cumulative trades: in this case, the calculation is made taking into account the change in the OI of only market trades that took place in the feed, and the direction is determined by the difference in the change in open interest up or down, respectively.

data.name: OIAnalyzerDescription
data.value: OI Analyzer combines two market indicators Bid/Ask and Open Interest (OI). Each of these indicators is a very powerful instrument for market analysis, but when used together, they create a unique indicator that allows you to see the volume of opening / closing positions of buyers and sellers separately in certain price zones. Important! This indicator only works for futures on the Russian market, as well as on the Binance and Bybit exchanges. For other instruments, OI data is not transmitted in real-time. The indicator only operates within the current trading day, which is done to optimize server load.

data.name: OIAnalyzerSeparatedTradesModeDescription
data.value: Separate trades: in this case, the real change in open interest is taken into account separately for ask and bid trades, both market orders and limit orders combined with them.

data.name: OISettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for an element of open interest.

data.name: OnBarCloseDescription
data.value: If this property is checked, the action occurs when the bar closes, otherwise instantly.

data.name: OneSelectionPerBarDescription

data.name: OpenInterestDescription
data.value: This indicator shows the total number of open short and long positions for the contract held by all market participants for this instrument. Note: The open interest is broadcast online only in the Russian market, and only for futures. Foreign stock exchanges cannot configure it on a real time basis because not all of them submit OI data online. Some stock exchanges do not submit OI at all. Crypto exchanges Binance and ByBit provide OI approximately once per minute(only for Futures)

data.name: OrderBlockIndDescription
data.value: The indicator forms zones around reversal candles: the boundaries of the bullish block are determined by the last falling candle, and the bearish block by the last rising candle. A reversal candle can be a candle with an extreme high/low for the period or the one preceding it.

data.name: OrderBookAlertsIndDescription
data.value: The indicator analyzes the order book within a specified number of levels and sends alerts when the volume of the found level is higher than the specified one.

data.name: OrderBookGroupDescription
data.value:
Peek into the market’s mind.
The "Order Book" category includes:

  • Tools to analyze the order book and identify hidden levels of interest.
  • Transparency into the intentions of market makers and major participants.
  • The ability to spot potential turning points before they happen.

Your ability to read the market like an open book starts here.

data.name: OrderFlowLinkingToBarDescription
data.value: If enabled, drawing of the order flow starts from the last bar, otherwise from the right edge of the chart.

data.name: OrderFlowRhythmDescription

data.name: OrderFlowSizeDescription

data.name: OrderSpeedIntervalDescription
data.value: Parameter used to calculate the speed of buying and selling activities. It is set in milliseconds. By adjusting this parameter, you can fine-tune the calculation of buying and selling speed to better reflect the current market situation.

data.name: OrderStatesError
data.value: Some orders have been sent to the broker server, but the response wasn't received. Perhaps, there is a connection issue. Do you really want to send this command? In case of connection issue, the value of position can vary from the real value.

data.name: OSMADescription
data.value: The Moving Average of Oscillator (OsMA) indicator is a variation of the MACD indicator and belongs to leading indicators, since its buy or sell signals come earlier than in other indicators. It is used to confirm a trend reversal or correction. The chart of the Moving Average of Oscillator indicator is a histogram which allows to display overbought and oversold conditions in the market. Since the indicator is identical to the MACD indicator, it is believed that their combined use gives a greater effect. The Moving Average of Oscillator (OsMA) technical indicator is the difference between the oscillator and the oscillator smoothing. In this case, the main MACD line is used as an oscillator, and the signal line is used as smoothing.

data.name: OsNotSupported

data.name: OutdatedVersionMessage
data.value: The client version is out of date

data.name: OutsideBarDescription
data.value: The Outside Bar is identified by bars having a high surpassing the high of the preceding bar and a low that descends below the low of the preceding bar.

data.name: OverboughtLimitDescription
data.value: Defines the upper limit above which downward movement can be expected.

data.name: OversoldLimitDescription
data.value: Defines the lower limit below which upward movement can be expected.

data.name: ParabolicSARDescription
data.value: SAR (Parabolic SAR) is a technical indicator that was developed to analyze trend markets. The indicator is shown on a price chart. In essence, this indicator is similar to the moving average, the only difference is that Parabolic SAR moves at a more accelerated rate (AF – acceleration factor) and may change its position in relation to the price. On the Up Trend, the indicator is below prices, and on the Down Trend, it is above them.

data.name: PenSettingsDescription
data.value: Specifies settings for the color, style, and width of the displayed lines.

data.name: PercentagePriceDescription
data.value: Percentage Price Oscillator is calculated as the difference between the short and long EMAs, divided by the long EMA. In comparison with MACD, Percentage Price Oscillator shows signals of divergences between prices and the oscillator value and highlights price extremes more accurately, its movements are more dynamic. The values are calculated by the following formula: (FastEMA-SlowEMA)/SlowEMA

data.name: PercentColorDescription
data.value: Sets the color depending on the percentage value.

data.name: PeriodDescription
data.value: Specifies the quantity of recent candles to include in the calculations.

data.name: PeriodMergeSwitchHotKeysDescription
data.value: Specifies a hotkey that, when activated, merges the period element indicated by the cursor with the following one.

data.name: PeriodsCountFilterDescription
data.value: If enabled, determines the number of periods to use, otherwise all possible periods are used.

data.name: PeriodSplitSwitchHotKeysDescription
data.value: Specifies a hotkey that, when activated, splits merged period elements located in the area indicated by the cursor.

data.name: PeriodTypeDescription
data.value: Allows you to select one of the provided period types.

data.name: PeriodValueDefinerDescription
data.value: A numeric value that serves to determine the period in indicator calculations.

data.name: PipsFromHighDescription
data.value: This property sets the maximum distance in ticks from the candle's high price that a level can be considered as valid. If the distance between the level and the candle's high price exceeds the value of this property the level will not be considered as valid.

data.name: PipsFromLowDescription
data.value: This property sets the maximum distance in ticks from the candle's low price that a level can be considered as valid. If the distance between the level and the candle's low price exceeds the value of this property the level will not be considered as valid.

data.name: PivotLevelColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color of the pivot level.

data.name: PivotsDescription
data.value: The Pivots indicator is a technical analysis tool used in trading to identify potential support and resistance levels on a price chart. It calculates and plots various pivot points, support levels, resistance levels, and midpoints based on the previous period high, low, and close prices. The indicator also provides flexibility by allowing users to customize the time period for pivot calculations, choose different formulas for pivot point calculations, and specify a custom trading session.

data.name: PlatformSubTitle
data.value: Premium platform for orderflow and volume analysis

data.name: PlatformTimeAdjustedDaylight
data.value: The time has been adjusted due to the daylight saving time change

data.name: PlatformTimeSetToLocal
data.value: The platform is set to the local time. You can change the settings in the "Time Zones" section.

data.name: PlaybookDescription
data.value: A playbook is a trading strategy or setup that you use repeatedly. Tag your trades with playbooks to track performance by strategy and identify what works best for you.

data.name: PlaybooksDescription
data.value: Create and track performance of your trading strategies

data.name: PocAlert
data.value: Alert when the price touches the POC level

data.name: POCLineSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the POC line.

data.name: PolarizedFractalDescription
data.value: Polarized Fractal Efficiency is a technical indicator, which shows the efficiency of market prices at the current moment. The indicator values range from 100 to -100. It is thought that if the indicator value is greater than 0, then this asset is characterized by an upward trend. If the indicator value is less than 0, then the asset is characterized by a downward trend. It is recommended to open a buy position, when the indicator value is below -50. It is recommended to open a sell position, when the indicator value is above 50.

data.name: PullbackOffsetModeDescription

data.name: QQEDescription
data.value: The Qualitative Quantitative Estimation (QQE) indicator works like a smoother version of the popular Relative Strength Index (RSI) indicator. QQE expands on RSI by adding two volatility based lines. These lines are composed of a fast and a slow moving Average True Range (ATR).

data.name: QStickDescription
data.value: The Q Stick indicator was developed by Tushar Chande for using with the candlestick chart as an additional method of measuring buy or sell strength in the market. The Q Stick indicator is built by calculating the moving average of the difference between the open and close prices over a certain period. A positive Q Stick value indicates buy strength and shows a net price upward movement over the specified time period, a negative value indicates that there is sell pressure and a net price downward movement during the period.

data.name: RatioDescription
data.value: Ratio - relation between two numbers. The ratio of numbers A and B can be expressed as A:B. Ratio Number appears above bearish candle and below bullish candle. Two numbers which are compared are the two highest asks for bearish candle and to lowest bids for bullish candles. When the value of Ratio is a number between Low Ratio and Neutral Ratio - the Ratio number is black. When the value of Ratio number above Neutral Ratio - the Ratio number is blue.

data.value: Relative Volume is a powerful tool to understand whether volume flows are increasing or decreasing. In fact, this is the ratio of the volume that is at the moment to the ordinary that is the average volume at this time of day.

The mode allows you to significantly reduce the data loading time, but keep in mind that the generated ticks will differ from the stock ticks. The mode is best suited for analyzing candlestick formations and volumes.

data.name: RepulseDescription
data.value: The Repulse indicator measures and reflects the bullish or bearish pressure, connected with each candle, in the form of a curve. The indicator has one parameter: bounce period. All 4 candle prices are used for the calculation, that is why the price type for the indicator calculation cannot be changed. The recommended way of usage is simple: - Crossing the zero line up is interpreted as a buy signal - Crossing the zero line down is interpreted as a sell signal.

data.name: RithmicErrorQuestion
data.value: The Heartbeat notifications from Rithmic: {0} Rithmic connection reports about possible communication problems. The platform may not receive the actual values of positions and order statuses. This means that your actions may lead to the changing of the position size, which will not be reflected in the platform and, as a result, may cause uncontrolled financial results. We advise you to reconnect your Rithmic account before continuing to trade. More details about the Heartbeat messages from Rithmic: https://support.atas.net/en/knowledge-bases/2/articles/355-connecting-to-rithmic

data.name: RithmicHeartbeatError
data.value:
Do you really want to send this command?
Recently the platform has received heartbeat messages from Rithmic's servers. Perhaps, there is a connection issue. In case of connection issue, the value of position can vary from the real value. Messages:

data.name: RithmicRTraderIsNotConnected
data.value: You are using the RTrader plugin connection, but RTrader is not currently logged in. Please log on to RTrader in host mode or modify the connection settings to utilize a direct connection instead.

data.name: RithmicRTraderIsNotRunning
data.value: You are using the RTrader plugin connection, but RTrader is not currently running. Please launch RTrader in host mode or modify the connection settings to utilize a direct connection instead.

data.name: RMIDescription
data.value: The Relative Momentum Index (RMI) indicator is based on the RSI indicator. Besides, the indicator compares closing values of following candles with a past candle (distance x candle period). The higher the selected period is, the smoother indicator values become.

data.name: RMODescription
data.value: The RMO (Rahul Mohindar Oscillator) indicator is a technical analysis tool used in trading to identify potential trend reversals. It involves calculating various moving averages and identifying reversal patterns to generate buy and sell signals.

data.name: ROCDescription
data.value: Rate of Change – this indicator shows the price change in relation to the set number of periods back.

data.name: RolloverTypeExpirationDateDescription
data.value: This method uses official exchange-mandated expiration dates for contract rollovers.

data.name: RolloverTypeVolumeBasedCurrentEndDescription
data.value: This marks the end of effective trading period for the current contract.

data.name: RolloverTypeVolumeBasedNextStartDescription
data.value: This marks the start of effective trading period for the next contract.

data.name: RoundNrDescription
data.value: The Round Numbers indicator plots levels with a step indicated in the settings (in ticks) on the chart.

data.name: RSIDescription
data.value: RSI indicator serves for the determination of the current trend strength, as well as possible points of its turn. The RSI indicator compares the absolute value of the price rise for a specific time period with its decline level for the same period. The calculation result is shown in the form of a curve on the chart, with values ranging within 0 % to 100 %.

data.name: RTIndicatorDescription
data.value: A unique indicator that combines the range (size) and the trend (direction) of the market. It allows you to find the market reversal points. Indicator is recommended to be used on RangeX and RangeZ charts. The algorithm of this indicator is closed by agreement with the author and it is available only in our platform. It is based on a neural network, a self-learning system, that combines the Range (size) and trend (direction) of the market. It allows you to find the area of a market reversal. An arrow differs from a dot by an additional filter. We recommend using RTIndicator in combination with various Range chart options.

data.name: RulerObjDescription
data.value: This drawing object provides an extended version of the Measure object, with the ability to extend the information block beyond the specified area.

data.name: RunTutorialAgain
data.value: Run training on next platform launch

data.name: RVI1Description
data.value: This indicator is one of the varieties of the RVI (Relative Vigor Index) indicator. RVI V1 measures relative price strength by comparing the ratio of changes in closing prices to changes in opening prices over a given period of time. This implementation uses a four-bar period, where the calculation is based on the closing and opening prices of the last four candles.

data.name: RVI2Description
data.value: Relative Vigor Index V2. The calculations are similar to the first version of the RVI indicator. In the second version, smoothed values of the original indicator are used in the calculations.

data.name: RVIDescription
data.value: Relative Vigor Index or RVI indicator. The RVI indicator is calculated using the same system as the Stochastic oscillator. However, in contrast, it compares the closing level with the opening level, but not with the minimum price for a trade period. As a result, the vigor index represents an actual price change for a period to be regulated for the maximum range of this period, for example, an hour or a day.

data.name: RVIPeriodDescription
data.value: Sets the period for the "Relative Vigor Index".

data.name: RWIDescription
data.value: Random Walk Indicator (RWI) helps to identify whether a price fluctuation is random or it is a part of a big trend. Random Walk Indicator is a combination of two lines: red, which shows the downtrend strength, and green, which corresponds to the uptrend strength.

data.name: SafeSpreadForStopOrder
data.value: Safe spread for stop order price

data.name: SampleGroupDescription
data.value:
Examples for those building their own indicators.
The "Sample" category includes:

  • Basic templates and sample indicators with open code.
  • Clear demonstrations of methods, functions, and calculation logic.
  • A great starting point for exploring the API and creating your own tools.

If you want to understand how indicators work from the inside — start here.

data.name: ScalpingDOMDescription
data.value: This template is based on the Scalping DOM trading indicator which was developed for easy scalping.

data.name: ScalpingDOMFullDescription
data.value:
This template is based on the Scalping DOM trading indicator which was developed for easy scalping.

The template allows:
  • analyzing clusters in combination with the tape and volumes in the DOM
  • quickly entering and exiting positions, placing and canceling orders in one click and changing the trading volume using hotkeys
  • seeing pings of recent orders
  • setting the calculation mode for the price of your position in the indicator settings: FIFO or FILO
  • arranging charts of different tools side by side using the ATAS layout system. It helps create a convenient workspace that allows you to monitor a large number of tools.

data.name: SchaffTrendCycleDescription
data.value: The Schaff Trend Cycle (STC) is a charting indicator that is commonly used to identify market trends and provide buy and sell signals to traders. STC is a type of oscillator and is based on the assumption that, regardless of time frame, currency trends accelerate and decelerate in cyclical patterns.

data.name: SessionColorIndDescription
data.value: The following indicator allows to highlight the background of the chart for a set period of time, cyclically, every trading day. For example, by setting a couple of such indicators with different setup, we can highlight the trading sessions of the main trading platforms, and visualize when they start, end, and intersect with each other. It may be important to consider this during the intraday trading, as during the opening and closing of a day, higher trading activity and instrument volatility is often observed.

data.name: SmartDOMBasicExtendedDescription

data.name: SmartDOMCustomFPSDescription

data.name: SmartDOMDarkExtendedDescription

data.name: SmartDOMDarkWintNotesDescription
data.value: Dark template with note-taking directly in the order book. Handy for visual market commentary.

data.name: SmartDOMDarlDescription
data.value: Dark template with extended trade panel, daily profile, and order queue display. Suitable for traders preferring dark interfaces.

data.name: SmartDOMGrayDescription
data.value: Multifunctional grey template: shows current day’s volume profile and order queue. Good balance of detail and neutral design.

data.name: SmartDOMLightDescription
data.value: Light template with a basic info set. Ideal for those who prefer light themes.

data.name: SmartDOMLightExtendedDescription
data.value: Extended light template: includes notes, spreads, and queue displays. Perfect for light theme fans who need full functionality.

data.name: SmartDONLightBidAskDescription
data.value: Light template focusing on the bid/ask profile and order queues. Useful for quickly gauging buyer and seller pressure.

data.name: SmartTapeBasicDescription
data.value: A basic smart tape template shows the flow of aggregated prints. You can get detailed information about any trade’s content if you press the mouse scroll wheel on a trade.

data.name: SmartTapeMenuDescription
data.value: Open a new Smart Tape module

data.name: SmartTapeSeparatedTradesDescription
data.value: The template displays a flow of non-aggregated prints. Groups of prints that have the same background show aggregated trades.

data.name: SmaSetingsDescription
data.value: Settings for displaying the moving average on the chart.

data.name: SMMADescription
data.value: The Smooth Moving Average (SMMA) resembles the Simple Moving Average (SMA) in its aim to diminish noise. This indicator considers all prices, employing an extended look-back period. Unlike the SMA, old prices are retained in the calculation, but their impact on the Moving Average is minimal due to their low weighting. This approach reduces noise, curbing fluctuations and capturing the predominant trend.

data.name: SMMAPeriodDescription
data.value: Sets the period of the smoothed moving average (SMMA).

data.name: SmoothDescription
data.value: Smoothing period applied to the indicator values.

data.name: SmoothingModesDescription

data.name: SmoothingRatioDescription
data.value: Determines the smoothing ratio for a given element.

data.name: SmoothLineSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the smooth line.

data.name: SmoothPeriodFilterDescription
data.value: If enabled, the calculations use smoothing of a certain parameter using a given period.

data.name: SomeTradesHaveZeroVolume
