data.name: SourceDescription
data.value: Allows the user to select the data source for calculating the indicator. It can be specific candlestick prices like open, close, etc., or another indicator

data.name: SourceKnowledgeQuestion
data.value: How did you hear about us?

data.name: SourceTypeDescription
data.value: Represents one of the available data source types.

data.name: SpaceBetweenLabelsDescription
data.value: Defines the size of the distance between labels.

data.name: SpaceBetweenObjectsDescription
data.value: Sets the spacing value between displayed objects.

data.name: SpeedOfTapeAutoFilterDescription
data.value: If enabled, the filter value is calculated automatically, otherwise it is equal to the specified value.

data.name: SpeedOfTapeDescription
data.value: This indicator is designed to measure the speed of trading activity. It calculates the pace of various market events, such as volume, ticks, buys, sells, or delta, over a specified time period. The indicator can be configured to automatically filter out noise based on a Simple Moving Average (SMA).

data.name: SpreadVolumeDescription
data.value: This indicator is designed to visualize the volume traded at bid and ask prices over time, providing insights into market activity. It displays colored rectangles representing bid and ask volume at different price levels.

data.name: SPVODescription
data.value: The Simple Percentage Volume Oscillator shows the percentage difference between two moving averages of the trading volume for a given security.

data.name: SquareObjDescription
data.value: This drawing object allows you to display a square on the chart.

data.name: SqueezeMomentumDescription
data.value: Indicator provides information on using squeeze momentum to find entry points in the market. It explains how to interpret black and grey dots, indicating a decrease in volatility and the beginning of a price movement. It also describes how the change in color of the dots indicates a change in the price direction, with green representing bullish movement and red representing bearish movement. A universal indicator that allows you to see in advance the probability of a price shot - a squeeze, that helps to find entry points. - The black dots show that the market is preparing to sharp movement, there is a decrease in volatility. - Grey dots after black, mean the end of thecompressionof the pulse and the early start of the price shot. The change in color indicates a change in the direction of the price. - Light Green - bullish price direction (Price goes up). - Dark Green - moving into the bearish market (Start of price decline). - Light Red - bear price direction (Price goes down). - Dark Red - moving into a bullish market (Start of price growth).

data.name: StableMessage
data.value: The stable version is the most reliable platform version, which is released several times a year and is intended for regular use. Before its release, the stable version was thoroughly checked by the testing team and the ATAS community. It doesn’t have all new functions and fixes of the last or beta version.

data.name: StableVersionDescription
data.value: The stable version is receiving updates once or twice a year and the most tested

data.name: StackedImbalanceDescription
data.value: This indicator is designed to identify and visualize imbalances between supply and demand volumes at adjacent price levels. It provides insight into potential areas of interest for traders based on differences in volume. The magnitude of the imbalance is determined as a percentage of the compared volumes. For example, let's analyze the value of 30 ask and the value of the overweight of 350%, 350% of 30 = 105, in order for the cluster to be highlighted, the bid must be more than 105.

data.name: StackedLevelsDescription
data.value: Determines the number of consecutive price levels found during the calculation.

data.name: StarcBandsDescription
data.value: The STARC Bands indicator is an alternative to the standard Bollinger Bands indicator. The STARC Bands narrow in stable markets and widen as volatility rises. Unlike the Bollinger Bands, the STARC Bands do not use closing prices and standard deviation. This indicator use the average true range (ATR) for calculation, which gives more meaningful information about the volatility level.The optimum ATR period is 10.

data.name: StartDayDescription
data.value: The start day of the trading platform

data.name: StartDayGreaterEndDay
data.value: Start day greater than end day.

data.name: StartLineAsArrowDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display the start of the line as an arrow.

data.name: StartRateText
data.value: Please rate your experience with ATAS

data.name: StartTimeDescription
data.value: The start time of the trading platform

data.name: StartTimeFilterDescription
data.value: Defines the starting time for the time filter.

data.name: StdDevBandsDescription
data.value: Standard Deviation Bands is a technical indicator, which is based on the SMA and StdDev indicators. The indicator consists of two moving averages (High and Low) and a channel (the upper channel is the sum of the moving average High and StdDev, the lower channel is the difference between the moving average Low and StdDev).

data.name: StdDevDescription
data.value: Technical indicator of standard deviation (Standard Deviation, StdDev) measures the market volatility. This indicator characterizes the price range as to the moving average. If the indicator value is large, the market is volatile and bar prices are quite scattered as to the moving average. If the indicator value is small, the market is exposed to low volatility and bar prices are sufficiently near to the moving average.

data.name: StdDevPeriodDescription
data.value: Sets the period for the "Standard Deviation".

data.name: StdErrBandsDescription
data.value: The Standard Error Bands indicator calculates standard error bands around a linear regression line. Standard error bands are used to visually represent the volatility or dispersion of prices around a regression line.

data.name: StdPeriodDescription
data.value: Determines the period for calculating the standard deviation.

data.name: StepScaleDescription
data.value: This parameter sets the price scaling step when scrolling the mouse wheel.

data.name: StepSizeInTicksDescription
data.value: Defines the step size expressed in ticks.

data.name: StochasticDescription
data.value: Stochastic oscillator (stochastic, stochastics) – a technical analysis indicator, which indicates the current price position as to the price range for a specific period in the past. It is measured in percent.

data.name: StochasticDiNapoliDescription
data.value: The Preferred Stochastic - DiNapoli indicator is a variation of the Stochastic Oscillator that incorporates exponential moving averages (EMA) for smoothing. The Stochastic Oscillator measures the location of a security's close relative to its price range over a certain period. The DiNapoli version adds further smoothing using EMAs.

data.name: StochasticMomentumDescription
data.value: Stochastic Momentum is a momentum oscillator that measures the location of the close price relative to the midpoint of the high-low range over a specified period. It helps identify overbought and oversold conditions.

data.name: StochasticRsiDescription
data.value: Stochastic RSI – it is a technical analysis indicator, which is used for identifying overselling and overbuying areas. It is calculated with the help of maximum and minimum values of the RSI indicator for a selected period of time. Stochastic RSI - is an oscillator, which calculates a value within the range from 0 to 1, which is shown in the chart as a line.

data.name: StochasticRsiPeriodDescription
data.value: Sets the period of Stochastic RSI.

data.name: StochasticRsiRsiPeriodDescription
data.value: Sets the RSI period of Stochastic RSI.

data.name: StohasticPercentileDescription
data.value: Stochastic - Percentile is a technical oscillator, which is based on the price position at a given time interval. Last N of bars are arranged in ascending order, and the arranged number of the last bar is divided by the period value. After the oscillator calculation, the values are smoothed by the moving average.

data.name: StopLimitDescription
data.value: Stop Limit Orders become limit orders when a specified stop price is reached. Specify a trigger price to activate the order.

data.name: StopLossPercentDescription
data.value: Defines the size of the area in percent from the order execution price to the stop loss price.

data.name: StopLossTicksDescription
data.value: Defines the size of the area in ticks from the order execution price to the stop loss price.

data.name: StopMarketDescription
data.value: Stop Market Orders become market orders when a specified price is reached. Specify a trigger price to activate the order.

data.name: StopOrderSafeSpreadDescription
data.value: Indent for the order price from the stop price

data.name: StopOrdersAgreement
data.value: This connection doesn't allow to submit server-side Stop & StopLimit orders. These types of orders get emulated locally on your computer. It means that for correct execution of these orders the platform has to be constantly connected to your broker. If you get disconnected the orders will NOT BE executed.

data.name: StrategyStopped

data.name: StrongNegativeFlowColorDescription
data.value: Sets the color of an element that represents strong negative cash flow.

data.name: StrongPositiveFlowColorDescription
data.value: Sets the color of an element that represents strong positive cash flow.

data.name: SubaccountDescription
data.value: Leave empty to use main account

data.name: SubPeriodsCloseColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color for the sub-period closing area.

data.name: SubPeriodsOpenColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color for the sub-period opening area.

data.name: SubPeriodSplitModeHotKeysDescription
data.value: Specifies a hotkey that, upon activation, toggles the split mode for displaying sub-periods of TPO corresponding to the period over which the mouse cursor is positioned.

data.name: SuperTrendDescription
data.value: The Super Trend indicator is build upon on two indicators – ATR and CCI. CCI points the direction of trend movement, meanwhile ATR is used to determine the levels of indicator. Calculation formula of the Super Trend: UpperLevel=(High[i]+Low[i])/2+Multiplier*Atr(Period) LowerLevel=(High[i]+Low[i])/2-Multiplier*Atr(Period)

data.name: SupportColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color for the element displaying the support level or support zone.

data.name: SupportLevelDisplayDescription
data.value: Displays the support level from which the price can rebound upward.

data.name: SupportLineSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the specified support level line.

data.name: SwingHighLowDescription
data.value: The Swing High and Low indicator is used to display the expected reversal points. In the chart, the red line shows sell reversal points, while the green one shows buy reversal points.

data.name: SwingPointsMemoryDescription
data.value: Number of recent swing points to keep in memory for analysis

data.name: SWWMADescription
data.value: Sine-Wave Weighted Moving Average calculates the moving average of a given financial instrument's prices using a weighted approach based on the sine function.

data.name: SyntheticVixDescription
data.value:
The Synthetic Vix indicator is a synthetic representation of the VIX (Volatility Index). The VIX is often referred to as the "fear index" and is used to gauge the market's expectation of future volatility. This synthetic version calculates a value based on the highest closing prices within a specified period compared to the current low, providing a measure of relative volatility.
The indicator is based on Larry Williams’ article "Fix the VIX". Describing what markets do, Bernard Baruch put it best: “Markets fluctuate”. This idea is embodied in the Chicago Board Options Exchange (CBOE) volatility index, VIX. Introduced in 1993, it became a very popular way to measure market risk. The VIX, which derives from the implied volatility of stock index options, is intended to represent traders expectations of volatility over the next 30 days. Essentially, the VIX reflects investors fears: its high values are associated with high volatility conditions (and low market), while low values are associated with low volatility conditions (and market tops). Unfortunately, the VIX is calculated only for S& P 500, NASDAQ Composite and Dow Jones Industrial Average indices. What about other markets? Luckily, it can be duplicated for any market — treasury bonds, gold, silver, soy beans, and even single stocks — with a simple formula: VIX(t)=100*(Highest(Close(t),n)-Low(t))/Highest(Close(t),n)

data.name: SZMADescription
data.value: This moving average type is a variation of the SMA. The difference is that, when calculating the average value, zero values are excluded to avoid bias in the values.

data.name: T3IndDescription
data.value: The indicator was developed by Tim Tillson in 1998. The purpose of developing a new type of moving average is to improve noise filtration and reduce lagging - a disadvantage which is common to most moving averages. Multiple exponential smoothing of the price series is used for calculating. If the price crosses the rising T3 line from below, it is a buy signal; if the falling line is crossed from above, it is a sell signal. The indicator is based on a six-fold calculation of the exponential moving average.

data.name: TableActionConfirmationMessage
data.value: Are you sure you want to close/cancel positions and orders? This action cannot be undone.

data.name: TableCellWidthPixelsDescription
data.value: Sets the table cell width in pixels.

data.name: TableOffsetXDescription
data.value: Sets the x-axis offset of the table in pixels.

data.name: TapePatternDescription
data.value:
The indicator is designed to process and filter “raw” order stream, flowing into the market. It has very sensitive settings to compute different automatic download algorithms and positions unloading. Here are a few things Tape Patterns will enable you to do: - You will be able to detect max and min volume in the current prints chain - You will be able to use this volume to filter - You will be able to see time in milliseconds between the desired prints - You will see the number of ticks in range - You will be able to detect trade side (buy/sell). The indicator takes into account all this data and displays it on the chart.
IMPORTANT! To optimize the load on the server, the indicator data is displayed only for the current session.

data.name: TapeReadingDescription
data.value:
There are many methods and instruments that help traders analyze and predict price movements in the world of financial markets. Tape analysis is one of these methods. The tape is a stream of information about completed trades, displayed in real-time.
This article will explore various aspects of tape reading and its main characteristics. We will also study indicators that are based on the tape: Order Flow Indicator, Big Trades, and others. We will delve into the main settings and ways to apply these indicators for market analysis and trading decisions.
Let's start our fascinating journey into the world of Order Flow analysis and reveal all its secrets

data.name: TDSequentialDescription
data.value: The indicator displays signals of a potential trend reversal based on the analysis of the close of the current candle in relation to the close of 4 candles ago. When signal No. 9 is reached, there is a high probability of a trend reversal. The probability of a reversal increases if the number of signals reaches 13.

data.name: TechnicalIndicatorsGroupDescription
data.value:
Timeless classics trusted by millions of traders.
The "Technical indicators" category includes:

  • A library of hundreds of indicators — from moving averages to MACD, RSI, and unique custom tools.
  • Instruments to analyze trends, volatility, momentum, and more.
  • Flexibility to build any strategy — from simple to advanced.

If you're looking for versatile tools, this is your arsenal.

data.name: TEMADescription
data.value: Triple Exponential Moving Average is a moving average variation, which is calculated by the following formula: TEMA(t) = 3*EMA(x(t)) - 3*EMA(EMA(x(t))) + EMA(EMA(EMA(x(t)))).

data.name: TickChartDescription
data.value: The chart is based on the tape’s aggregated data, allowing you to see the dynamics of price movement in the short term. The volume chart at the bottom shows prints that were executed with slippage, which may indicate that the initiator is rather aggressive.

data.name: TMADescription
data.value: The main weight of Triangular Moving Averages falls on the middle part of the price range. In fact, they are double-smoothed simple moving averages. The length of simple moving averages depends on the parity of the selected number of periods.

data.name: ToDateLessFromDateErrorMesage

data.name: ToStayConnectedWithTheMarket

data.name: TPOAndProfileDescription
data.value: A classic market profile. Time Price Opportunity chart shows where price spent the most time — valuable hints for fair value assessment and spotting accumulation/distribution zones.

data.name: TPOAndProfileFullDescription
data.value:
A Profile & TPO chart. It visualizes stock data according to the Market Profile concept proposed by Peter Steidlmayer.

The chart shows:
  • Daily distributions of the TPO chart based on thirty-minute price movements.
  • Daily distributions of trading volume.
  • Point of control and value area prices within each day.

The TPO and Profiles indicator serves as a basis for the chart. The settings of this indicator allow you to customize visualization with great flexibility, show additional levels and extend them until they intersect with the price, split the TPO chart into subperiods and much more.

data.value: This indicator is designed to visualize transactions on the chart in the format of "opening a position - closing a position".

data.name: TradesTrackDescription
data.value: The indicator displays buys and sells in real time in the form of appearing and fading marks at the current price level.

data.name: TradingGroupDescription
data.value:
Trading in action — with every click.
The "Trading" category includes:

  • Functions and scripts that simplify decision-making.
  • Automation tools to enhance efficiency.
  • Time-saving features that let you focus on strategy.

Everything that helps you trade faster, smarter, and easier — all in one place.

data.name: TradingIsLockedMessage
data.value: Trading is locked. Use context menu to unlock.

data.name: TradingModuleInfo
data.value: Chart trading. If this mode is on, you can put limit and stop orders straight on the chart. Use left click for limit order, right click for stop order. The program will automatically choose the order direction. To quickly activate this mode, press space button.

data.name: TradingPanelIndDescription
data.value: The TradingPanel indicator is an interactive trading panel for the ATAS platform, allowing you to conveniently manage market and limit orders directly from the chart. The panel supports opening and closing positions at the market price, as well as using the "Chase" and "Pullback" strategies, automatically tracks and modifies active orders depending on changes in the best price. The visual interface is presented in the form of buttons for managing orders.

data.name: TradingSessionDescription
data.value: The trading session determines the period of instrument trading time for what the data should be displayed.

data.name: TrailingHelpText
data.value:
Trailing
Allows you to move the SL order when the current price moves in the direction of increasing profit for the position. If the price continues to move in the direction of increasing profit, the SL order can be moved multiple times.
Parameters:
• Trailing Stop - the value by which the price must move from the previous level for the SL order to be moved to a new level
• Trailing Step - the step with which the SL order will be moved relative to the previous SL order price

data.name: TrailingTooltip
data.value:
Allows moving SL order when the current price moves in the direction of increasing profit for the position. If the price continues moving towards profit, the SL order can be moved multiple times.
Parameters: • Trailing Stop — value by which the price must move from the previous level for the SL order to be moved to a new level • Trailing Step — step at which SL order will be moved relative to the previous SL order price

data.name: TrainingMinutesDescription
data.value: Training period for collecting historical data and calculating liquidity maximums. After training completes, values will be normalized against observed maximums.

data.name: TransaqLoggingLevel
data.value: Logging level for Transaq Connector library

data.name: TrendLineObjDescription
data.value: It is a graphical line on financial charts that connects key price points to show the direction of the trend.

data.name: TrendLineTypeDescription
data.value: Defines one of the available trendline types.

data.name: TrialErrorMessage1
data.value: It looks like you've already used your trial period

data.name: TrialErrorMessage2
data.value: If this is incorrect, contact our support team at:

data.name: TrialErrorMessage3_2
data.value: get a paid subscription and access all ATAS features.

data.name: TRIXDescription
data.value: TRIX (triple exponentially smoothed moving average) is an oscillator which represents a percentage of the triple exponentially smoothed moving average nearby values of closing prices for a certain period.

data.name: TrueRangeDescription
data.value: True Range is a technical indicator. It is calculated as the maximum among the following values: High(t) - Low(t), |High(t) - Close(t-1)|,|Low(t) - Close(t-1)|

data.name: TSIDescription
data.value: True Strength Index (TSI) is a technical indicator used in the financial market analysis. It uses moving averages of the main momentum of a financial instrument. The TSI indicator is based on the double smoothing of price changes. The true strength indicator fluctuates between positive and negative values, and the centerline of the indicator identifies the total offset. The Momentum is considered a leading indicator of price action, while the Moving Average follows the price. The TSI indicator combines these characteristics to indicate the price and direction which are more in sync with the market movements than a single momentum or moving average. It tries to show the trend direction, is useful for identifying oversold and overbought conditions in the market and warns of a weakening trend through divergence.

data.name: TVIDescription
data.value: The TVI (Trade Volume Index) is a simple indicator that attempts to quantify the relationship between price changes and volume at the tick level. It is commonly used to identify the strength of a price movement based on the tick-by-tick changes in price and volume.

data.name: UltimateOscillatorDescription
data.value: The Ultimate Oscillator is a technical indicator developed by Larry Williams in 1976 to measure the price momentum of an asset using multiple periods. Using a weighted average with three different periods, the indicator has less volatility and fewer trading signals compared to other oscillators using a single period.

data.name: UnderstandCommentLabel
data.value: What was not clear, what could be done better?

data.name: UnfinishedAuctionModDescription
data.value: Unfinished Auction - unfinished business. This term is used to describe a situation where the extreme price level (upper, lower, or both) contains both buys and sells at the same time. The indicator is used to search and display similar situations in the market. Important: if you are looking for a highlight at the low level, you need to have more bids at the low than the bid filter, and the number of asks should be greater than zero. If you are looking for a highlight at the high level, you need to have more asks at the high than the ask filter, and the number of bids should be greater than zero.

data.name: UpAlertFileFilterDescription
data.value: When activated, an alert is triggered in the presence of a ascending signal, utilizing the specified sound file name.

data.name: UpAreaSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the rising area.

data.name: UpArrowObjDescription
data.value: This drawing object allows you to display a up arrow on the chart.

data.name: UpDownVolumeRatioDescription
data.value: The indicator calculates the average moving up/down ratio. Calculations are based on bid and ask volumes or on bullish and bearish bar volumes.

data.name: UpperCutOffDescription
data.value: The parameter sets the upper limit for the maximum volume, determining the colors used to represent it. For example, if a 5% cutoff is selected, the platform will assign the maximum color to the top 5% of volumes. This feature works with the Delta, Volume Proportion, and Trades Proportion color schemes.

data.name: UpperCutOffDescriptionHeatmap
data.value: The parameter sets the upper limit for the maximum volume, determining the colors used to represent it. For example, if a 5% cutoff is selected, the platform will assign the maximum color to the top 5% of volumes.

data.name: UpperDeviationSettingsDescription

data.name: UpperPositiveValueColorDescription

data.name: UpperWickSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the upper wick element.

data.name: UseAlertBeforeDescription

data.name: UseAlertDescription

data.name: UseAlertsDescription
data.value: Enables or disables the sound notification.

data.name: UseAlternateEndpointBybitDescription
data.value: Check to use api.bytick.com. Uncheck for api.bybit.com. This applies only to mainnet. This could be useful if you got banned permanently.

data.name: UseCrossAlertDescription

data.name: UseCumTradesTimeFilterDescription
data.value: If activated, the calculation incorporates only cumulative trades where the time span between the conclusion and initiation of the trade does not surpass a predefined duration.

data.name: UseCustomDiapasonDescription
data.value: If enabled, a custom range of values is used when calculating the indicator, otherwise default values.

data.name: UseCustomUpdateIntervalsDescription
data.value: Use update frequency set by a user

data.name: UseFilterDescription
data.value: Determines whether to use this filter in calculations.

data.name: UseFuturesTestnetDescription
data.value: Use futures testnet api endpoints (Get special API keys from https://testnet.binancefuture.com/ . Check the 'API Keys' tab below the chart)

data.name: UseOnlyGuaranteedAveragePrice
data.value: Trade only with the guaranteed average price

data.name: UseOnlyGuaranteedAveragePriceDescription
data.value: In some cases, the platform cannot guarantee the average position price correctness (it concerns some trading connections such as Quik, SmartCom, Transaq, Plaza2, when not all trades, which are necessary for a trade calculation, have been received from the trading connection). Using strategies when the average price is not guaranteed can lead to placing protective orders at wrong prices. Disable this option if you understand and accept all the possible risks involved.

data.name: UseOptimizationModeDescription
data.value: Rounding chart bars and indicators lines on small chart scale

data.name: UsePartialGapClosureDescription
data.value: Determines whether to use partial gap closure.

data.name: UsePeriodDescription
data.value: If enabled, the specified period is used for indicator calculations.

data.name: UsePocChangeAlertDescription
data.value: Determines whether to use an alert when the POC level changes.

data.name: UsePocTouchAlertDescription
data.value: Determines whether to use an alert when the price touches the POC level.

data.name: UsePreviousCloseDescription
data.value: Determines if calculations should consider the closing price of the previous candle.

data.name: UseRepetitiveAlertDescription
data.value: Show the alert again on each repeated level touch

data.name: UserNotWhitelistedMessage
data.value: Unfortunately, your account is not on the allowlist, so access to the platform is currently unavailable. If you’d like to request access or think this might be an error, please reach out to our support team — we’ll be happy to help.

data.name: UseScaleDescription
data.value: When enabled, the line is fixed at the level specified in the "Value" field. When disabled, the line position is determined automatically.

data.name: UseSpotTestnetBinanceDescription
data.value: Use spot testnet api endpoints (Get special API keys from https://testnet.binance.vision/)

data.name: UseTestnetBybitDescription
data.value: Check to use testnet.bybit.com. Uncheck for mainnet.

data.name: UseTimeFilterDescription
data.value: This setting enables the application of a time-based filter for the indicator. When activated, the indicator will only analyze and display data for candles that fall within a specified time range. If turned off, the indicator considers candles from all time frames.

data.name: UseTrueRangeDescription
data.value: If this option is enabled, the true range is used, otherwise the High-Low candlestick size is used.

data.name: UseVAHTouchAlertDescription
data.value: Determines whether to use an alert when the price touches the VAH level.

data.name: UseVALTouchAlertDescription
data.value: Determines whether to use an alert when the price touches the VAL level.

data.name: VAHLineSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the VAH line.

data.name: VALLineSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the VAL line.

data.name: ValueAreaCacheDelayDescription
data.value: In case of frequent updates of a candle this setting allow to reduce CPU usage. Recommended range is 100 - 1000. The more the value the slower value area update and better perfomance.

data.name: ValueAreaLinesColor
data.value: Сolor of the lines in the value area

data.name: ValueAreaStepDescription
data.value: How much levels to take at a single step of calculation. Usual values are 1 or 2

data.name: ValueAreaVisualTypeDescription
data.value: Allows you to select one of the available types of display of the value area.

data.name: ValueDataSeriesColorDescription
data.value: Defines the color used to display the series on the chart.

data.name: ValueDataSeriesTextColorDescription
data.value: Defines the text color used to display the series on the chart.

data.name: ValueDataSeriesVisualTypeDescription
data.value: Specifies the style for displaying data series on a chart.

data.name: ValueFilterDescription
data.value: Defines the filter value for internal calculations.

data.name: ValuesRangeDescription
data.value: Specifies the minimum allowed value and the maximum allowed value.

data.name: VBRRDescription
data.value: Volume Bar Range Ratio displays the ratio of bar volume to bar height. The formula of the indicator calculation: VBBR(t) = Vol(t) / (high(t) - Low(t))

data.name: VerticalHorizontalFilterDescription
data.value: The vertical horizontal filter evaluates whether prices are currently trending or in a consolidation phase. The calculation uses the ratio of the difference between the maximum and minimum values to the sum of the values for a certain period, taking into account the selected data source.

data.name: VerticalLineObjDescription
data.value: This drawing object allows you to place a vertical line on the selected chart, extending from the upper to the lower border and located on a specific bar.

data.name: VidyaDescription
data.value: The Volumatic VIDYA (Variable Index Dynamic Average) indicator is a trend-following tool that calculates and visualizes both the current trend and the corresponding buy and sell pressure within each trend phase.

data.name: VisibleProportion
data.value: Proportion by visible part of the chart

data.name: VisibleProportionDescription
data.value: Color highlighting of data in the current window only.

data.name: VisualizationModeDescription
data.value: Specifies one of the available visualization modes.

data.name: VisualModeBlockDescription
data.value: Presents data as a square block on each bar

data.name: VisualModeCrossDescription
data.value: Places a cross on each bar

data.name: VisualModeDescription
data.value: The visual mode setting determines how selections are displayed on the chart

data.name: VisualModeDotsDescription
data.value: Depicts data as a circle on each bar

data.name: VisualModeDownArrowDescription
data.value: Represents data points with a downward arrow on each bar

data.name: VisualModeHashDescription
data.value: Displays a horizontal line on each bar

data.name: VisualModeHideDescription
data.value: Hides the data series from the chart

data.name: VisualModeHistogramDescription
data.value: Shows a vertical column on each bar

data.name: VisualModeLineDescription
data.value: Connects data points with a line

data.name: VisualModeOnlyValueOnAxisDescription
data.value: Shows values only on the axis, without connecting lines or shapes

data.name: VisualModeSquareDescription
data.value: Uses a 90-degree polyline connecting data points

data.name: VisualModeUpArrowDescription
data.value: Indicates data points with an upward arrow on each bar

data.name: VisualObjectsDescription
data.value: This setting allows users to customize the color of the visual objects on the chart.

data.name: VisualObjectsTransparencyDescription
data.value: This setting allows users to customize transparency of the visual objects on the chart.

data.name: VolatilityChaikinsDescription
data.value: Chaikin Volatility is a simple and convenient indicator used to determine the market volatility. The instrument measures the difference between the maximum and minimum prices in a certain time range.

data.name: VolatilityHistDescription
data.value: It is one of the volatility types. It is calculated on the basis of past data for a certain period of time. In this indicator, the historical volatility is calculated as the standard deviation.

data.name: VolatilityTrendDescription
data.value: Volatility Trend is an indicator which uses peak price values for an indicated period, as well as the ATR indicator values, when calculating. The values indicate a supposed short-term trend.

data.name: VolumeAnalysisDelta
data.value: Volume analysis: delta, cumulative delta, bid-ask imbalance, VWAP

data.name: VolumeHistogramsDescription
data.value: Maximum insight with minimal visual noise. Horizontal volume histograms to assess market activity across price levels.

data.name: VolumeHistSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the volume histogram.

data.name: VolumeIndDescription
data.value: The indicator displays the number of traded volumes on each chart candle.

data.name: VolumeIndexDescription
data.value: NVI (Negative Volume Index) and PVI (Positive Volume Index) are technical indicators with a mirror-like calculation methodology, but with a similar interpretation, accumulating price changes for certain periods with, respectively, falling and growing trading volume relative to the previous period. The volume index is equal to the cumulative sum of the current profit multiplied by the index value in the previous period at the moments when the current day's trading volume is less (or more) than the previous day's trading volume.

data.name: VolumeLabelDescription
data.value: Show volume values on bars (in cluster mode)

data.name: VolumeMAPeriodDescription
data.value: Represents the period for the volume moving average.

data.name: VolumeMoreNBars
data.value: Volume is more that N last bars total volume

data.name: VolumeMoreSma
data.value: Volume is more than SMA(N) volume

data.name: VolumeOnChartDescription
data.value: The indicator is a complete analogue of the Volume indicator, except that it is displayed not in a separate panel, but at the bottom of the chart.

data.name: VolumeOrderFlowGroupDescription
data.value:
Market activity analysis at the trade level.

  • Real-time volumes, delta, and order flow.
  • Work with clusters, imbalances, and volume concentration levels.
  • Assess buyer and seller activity in key price zones.

data.name: VolumePerTradeIndDescription
data.value: The indicator shows the average trade volume.

data.name: VolumeProfileTemplateDescription
data.value: Identify true support and resistance levels using the volume profile to find the best entry points for trades.

data.name: VolumeSettingsDescription
data.value: Visualization settings for the volume elements.

data.name: VolumesInUSDTDescription
data.value: This setting displays the contents of cluster volumes, limit orders in the order book and some indicators, in the quote currency for USDT pairs.

data.name: VolumeSupResZonesDescription
data.value: This indicator relies on significant trading volume at fractal lows or highs, and the zones are determined by the length of the candle wick for that particular time frame. This helps traders identify critical price levels that play an important role in either reversing a trend or maintaining its continuation when zones are broken and subsequently retested. It is possible to use up to 4 different periods simultaneously.

data.name: VolumeTrendDescription
data.value: The Price Volume Trend (PVT) indicator, a momentum-based tool used in technical analysis to gauge money flow. The PVT calculates changes in volume by adding or subtracting a portion of the daily volume based on the percentage change in the current day's price compared to the previous day's close. This indicator can confirm trends and identify potential trading signals through divergences.

data.name: VolumeTypeDescription
data.value: Determines which volume type to use.

data.name: VolumeZoneIndDescription
data.value: This indicator aims to analyze price movements in relation to volume changes. It calculates a ratio of the Exponential Moving Average (EMA) of volume for up and down price movements. This ratio is then normalized to a percentage scale, providing insights into potential overbought or oversold conditions.

data.name: VortexIndDescription
data.value: This indicator calculates and presents Positive Vortex and Negative Vortex based on current high/low/close prices and previous ones.

data.name: VPFDescription
data.value: The Voss Predictive Filter by John Ehlers is a new filter indicator that could help signal cyclical turning points in markets. As described by the author, the filter has a negative group delay and while an indicator based on it cannot actually see into the future, it may provide the trader with signals in advance of other indicators.

data.name: VsaBetterVolumeDescription
data.value: The VSA Better Volume indicator uses the current tick volume together with the candle range - the spread, comparing them with the previous values. Description of signals: ● red. Large volume + bullish candle with a large spread. The signal appears when an uptrend starts and ends, as well as during correction (pullbacks) in a bear market. ● white. Large volume + bearish candlestick with a large spread. The signal appears at the origin and end of a downtrend, also during correction (pullbacks) in a bull market. ● green. Large volume + candlestick with a small spread. As a rule, it occurs at the end of a trend movement, when the market maker “dumps” his trades to ordinary traders, who will incur losses, since players open their positions at the end of the trend, just before the price reverses, and also at the time of fixing profits on corrections ( in the middle of a trend movement). ● magenta. A group of candles with high volume. Candlesticks have large and small spreads. This signal rarely appears, it usually occurs on a correction (rollback). ● yellow. Small tick volume candles. A common occurrence at the end of a trend and during a correction. ● blue. "Standard" situation when there are no signals (like described for other colors).

data.name: VsaWsdDescription
data.value: The indicator is designed to find divergence between volume and price. It processes tick volume data and shows the analysis value on a color chart. In addition to volumes VSA - WSD Histogram can find support and resistance levels, which are calculated based on supply and demand values and candlestick range. On the indicator histogram for each bar, the spread is the difference between the high and low of the bar and marked with white.

data.name: VWAPDescription
data.value: VWAP indicator is a volume-weighted average price. The VWAP is displayed directly on the price chart. The indicator calculation starts when the trading session is opened and continues until it is closed, i. e. during one day. Hence, the VWAP is mainly intended for trading during one day. To calculate the indicator, the cumulative value of the product of the volume by the typical price should be divided by the cumulative volume.

data.name: VWMADescription
data.value: The Volume Weighted Moving Average (VWMA) Indicator is a sophisticated technical analysis tool that calculates the average price of an asset while taking into account the volume traded at each price level.

data.name: WADDescription
data.value: The Accumulation / Distribution - Williams indicator is the sum of upward price movements, which are the result of accumulation of securities by the market participants and downward movements, which are caused by distribution of securities, or, simply speaking, by their selling.

data.name: WaitEndOfOperation
data.value: Wait until the end of current operation.

data.name: WaitPreviousPlatformClosing
data.value: Please wait, the previous platform instance is closing...

data.name: WantUseAtasSim
data.value: I also want to trade on ATAS demo account

data.name: WAODescription
data.value: This oscillator shows the difference between two weighted moving averages with different periods.

data.name: WarningMarketDataLatencyMessage
data.value: Attention, there is a delay in Market data of {0:F0} ms.

data.name: WarningOrdersLatencyMessage
data.value: Attention, there is an order delay of {0:F0} ms.

data.name: WarningTimeSinceLastMarketDataReceivedMessage
data.value: The last update of Market data was {0}.

data.name: WatchEditorButtonsDescription
data.value:
Button [Apply] - will apply the settings to the open position.
Button [Save] - will save the settings and apply them to the next position.

data.name: WatchlistNameDescription
data.value: Watchlist name which will be displayed in the module header. If more than one watchlist is used, the name will help distinguish them.

data.name: WatchModeDescription
data.value: SL/TP order values ​​have been changed when trading with an open position. After the position is closed, the SL/TP order settings will return to the previous settings that were before the position was opened.

data.name: WatchModeTitle
data.value: The strategy is in Watch mode (SL / TP were changed manually)

data.name: WatermarkIndDescription
data.value: Sample of indicator that shows a watermark on a chart. It is also possible to display custom text.

data.name: WavetrendDescription
data.value: An oscillator that shows price changes and identifies areas of overbought and oversold. It can also show trend reversal points.

data.name: WeakNegativeFlowColorDescription
data.value: Sets the color of an element that represents weak negative cash flow.

data.name: WeakPositiveFlowColorDescription
data.value: Sets the color of an element that represents weak positive cash flow.

data.name: WeightDecayDescription
data.value: Exponential weight decay parameter. Lower values give more weight to levels near the market, higher values distribute weights more evenly across depth.

data.name: WeissWaveDescription
data.value: The Weis Wave indicator is used to display the total volume of the trend before changing its direction. That is, the indicator summarizes the volume of candles of the same direction, which go in a row.

data.name: WhatIsGoingOnPrompt
data.value: What is going on in this chart? Analyze the price action, trends, and provide insights about the market behavior.

data.name: WhitebitMarginTypeRestricted
data.value: Margin type change is not supported by Whitebit

data.name: WidthDataSeriesDescription
data.value: Indicator line thickness. Applies only in "Line" display mode.

data.name: WilliamsRDescription
data.value: Williams’ Percent Range, Williams’ %R is a technical indicator which helps identify overbought or oversold conditions in the market and give divergence signals indicating a market reversal. Williams%R is the ratio between the difference between the high and the closing price, and the «high-low» range for a certain period, multiplied by 100. %R = (max(High(n)) — Close(t)) / (max(High(n)) — min(Low(n))) For convenience and clarity, the indicator has a value reversal function.

data.name: WMADescription
data.value: WMA( Weighted moving average) is the exponential value also gives weight to later data, but makes this in a more explicit and simpler. When calculating the 5-day weighted moving average, we give the five-fold weight to the current price, the four-fold weight to the yesterday’s price, and the three-fold weight to the price on the day before yesterday, etc. Then the sum of all the products is divided by the added weight sum.

data.name: WMAPeriodDescription
data.value: Sets the period of the weighted moving average (WMA).

data.name: WoodiesCCIDescription
data.value: Woodies CCI indicator is the modified conventional CCI (Commodity Channel Index). The indicator is composed of two CCI, with the periods of 6 and 14. A fast curve is used to forecast the movement of a slow one. The Woodies CCI is also supplemented by a histogram for the slow CCI, which has different colors depending on the conditions. Values of the Woodies CCI index vary near the zero line they have neither upper, nor lower boundaries. The high value of the indicator means the “overbought” price at present; the low value means the “oversold” price. The key levels of the Woodies CCI index are +-100, +-200 and zero line.

data.name: WorkspaceAlredyExistsLabel
data.value: A workspace named {0} already exists in the list. Select action:

data.name: WorkspaceFileDamagedCanLoadMessage
data.value: The workspace is damaged, some windows may not load correctly. Continue?

data.name: WorkspaceHasManyModulesMessage
data.value: There are too many modules opened in the workspace. We recommend you not to open more than 30 charts, 45 Smart DOMs and more than 60 modules in total. To speed up the workspace loading, we recommend closing unused modules.

data.name: WorkspaceHasModulesWithProblemsMessagePart1
data.value: Several modules which are not optimally configured have been found in this workspace. Such modules are marked with a sign

data.name: WorkspaceHasModulesWithProblemsMessagePart2
data.value: You can get detailed information and recommendations for fixing, if you hover over this sign. Also, you can fix everything by clicking here:

data.name: WorkspaceHasNotSupportedInstruments
data.value: The workspace {0} has instruments that are not supported by the license used. Unsupported instruments will not be loaded. Continue?

data.name: WorkspaceHasProblemsToolTip
data.value: Problems have been found in the workspace. To fix these problems, go to the workspace editor.

data.name: WorkspaceMayBeLongLoadToolTip
data.value: The selected workspace can take a long time to load due to the large number of open modules. You can close unnecessary modules in the workspace editor.

data.name: WPRDescription
data.value: The WPR indicator (Williams’ Percent Range or Williams Overbought / Oversold Index) was invented by Larry Williams to estimate the speed of price movements and shows the position of the closing price relative to the max / min levels for a certain period. The indicator shows the level of the current closing price of the period relative to the real range (max / min). Values (in percent) are displayed in the range from 0% to (-100%).

data.name: WrongBrokerMessage
data.value: Unfortunately, connection through this broker is not supported. In order to continue trading through our platform on this exchange, we recommend using the services of our broker partner WhiteList, which provides access to this exchange, as well as a discount of up to 30% when trading.

data.name: WrongBrokerRefCodeMessage
data.value: Dear user, unfortunately, connection through this broker is only possible if you indicate our referral code in your personal account. In order to continue trading through our platform, we recommend informing technical support of the WhiteList broker that you want to change your referral code to: ATAS

data.name: WrongRefCodeMessage
data.value: Dear user, unfortunately, your account has a referral code that does not belong to ATAS. In order to continue trading through our platform on this exchange, we recommend using the services of our broker partner WhiteList, which provides access to this exchange, as well as a discount of up to 80% on commission.

data.name: WWMADescription
data.value: Welles Wilders Moving Average employs Wilder's smoothing approach, assigning higher importance to recent data. The calculation entails deducting the prior average from the current price and incorporating the difference into the previous average. This yields a moving average with swifter responsiveness compared to conventional methods.

data.name: YearsInTradingQuestion
data.value: How many years have you been trading?

data.name: YouHaveActiveSLTP
data.value: You have active positions with active SL/TP ( {0} )

data.name: YouHaveStartedStrategies
data.value: You have {0} active ATM or chart strategies.

data.name: YourAccountWillExpireWithin1Day
data.value: Your license will expire in a day.

data.name: YourAccountWillExpireWithin2Days
data.value: Your license will expire in 2 days.

data.name: YourAccountWillExpireWithin3Days
data.value: Your license will expire in 3 days.

data.name: YourLicenseAlreadyExpired
data.value:
Your license has expired
To renew it, choose the appropriate tariff in your Personal Area

data.name: YourWorkspaceLimitedTitle
data.value: The workspace being loaded contains elements not available in your plan

data.name: ZigzagIndDescription
data.value: The Zig Zag indicator plots points on the chart whenever prices reverse by a percentage greater than a pre-chosen variable. Straight lines are then drawn, connecting these points. The indicator is used to help identify price trends. The ZigZag also gives you information about these moves: Volume, Delta, Candle count and Time.

data.name: ZLEMAIndDescription
data.value: The Zero Lag Exponential Moving Average indicator was created by John Ehlers and Rick Way. As with the Double Exponential Moving Average (DEMA) and Triple Exponential Moving Average (TEMA), and as the name suggests, the goal is to eliminate the lag, which is inherent in all trend-following indicators and averages the price over time.

data.name: ZoneDirectionDescription
data.value: Defines one of the available directions for the zone.

data.name: ZScoreIndDescription
data.value: Z-score (Standard score) is a measure of the relative spread of an observed or measured value which indicates how many standard deviations its relative mean spread is. The indicator displays the value of the price deviation from its average value.

data.name: ShowThresholdLinesDescription
data.value: Show horizontal threshold lines in the Volume panel.

data.name: ThresholdSourceDescription
data.value: Select fixed thresholds or dynamic Welford thresholds.

data.name: SessionWindowModeDescription
data.value: Session window used to reset dynamic thresholds.

data.name: RthStartDescription
data.value: Start time of Regular Trading Hours (exchange time).

data.name: RthEndDescription
data.value: End time of Regular Trading Hours (exchange time).

data.name: SamplesForMeanStdDescription
data.value: Minimum samples required before dynamic thresholds become active.

data.name: LabelFillColorDescription
data.value: Sets the background color of the label.

data.name: ShowVolumePerSecondDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display information about volume per second.

data.name: ShowDeltaDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display information about delta.

data.name: ShowDeltaPerVolumeDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display information about delta per volume.

data.name: ShowDeltaChangeDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display information about the difference between the delta of the current candle and the delta of the previous one.

data.name: ShowMaximumDeltaDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display information about maximum delta.

data.name: ShowMinimumDeltaDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display information about minimum delta.

data.name: ShowVolumesDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display volume information.

data.name: ShowTradesCountDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display information about the number of trades.

data.name: ShowCandleHeightDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display information about the height of the candle (in currency).

data.name: ShowCandleDurationDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display information about the time duration of the candle.

data.name: ShowCandleTimeDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display information about the start time of the candle.

data.name: ShowSessionVolumeDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display cumulative volume information within a session.

data.name: ShowSessionDeltaDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display information about session delta.

data.name: ShowSessionDeltaPerVolumeDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display information about session delta per volume.

data.name: ShowAsksDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display information about ask volumes.

data.name: ShowBidsDescription
data.value: Determines whether to display information about bid volumes.

data.name: UseAlertDescription
data.value: This setting enables alert notifications for specific conditions or events as defined by the indicator. When activated, the indicator will send an alert.

data.name: AlertFilterDescription
data.value: Sets the value at which the alert is triggered.

data.name: AlertFileDescription
data.value: This parameter allows users to specify the name of a custom audio file for alert sounds. Users can select a WAV file that will be played whenever an alert condition is triggered. The chosen WAV file should be located in the folder C:\Program Files (x86)\ATAS Platform\Sounds.

data.name: OverrideColorInSchemesDescription
data.value: If enabled, this line will use its own Color even when visual semantic schemes are active.

data.name: OverrideWidthInSchemesDescription
data.value: If enabled, this level will use its own line width even when visual semantic schemes are active.

data.name: OverrideStyleInSchemesDescription
data.value: If enabled, this level will use its own line style even when visual semantic schemes are active.

data.name: VisualMode_Ruleset_Description
data.value: Visualization driven by semantic rules and palettes.

data.name: HVN_ThresholdPct_Description
data.value: A price level is considered HVN when its volume is at or above this percentage of the profile POC volume.

data.name: HVN_GapToleranceTicks_Description
data.value: Allows small gaps (in ticks) inside an HVN band to keep nearby high-volume levels grouped.

data.name: HVN_OcclusionTicks_Description
data.value: Minimum vertical distance (in ticks) required between HVN bands of different periods to avoid visual overlap.

data.name: LVN_ThresholdPct_Description
data.value: A price level is considered LVN when its volume is below this percentage of the profile POC volume.

data.name: LVN_GapToleranceTicks_Description
data.value: Allows small gaps (in ticks) inside an LVN band to keep nearby low-volume levels grouped.

data.name: LVN_OcclusionTicks_Description
data.value: Minimum vertical distance (in ticks) required between LVN bands of different periods to avoid visual overlap.

data.name: LVN_MinPocVol_Description
data.value: Do not compute LVN bands until the profile POC volume reaches this value. Prevents LVN noise early in the period.

data.name: LVN_TailFilterMinTicks_Description
data.value: Excludes low-volume tail areas near the high and low of the profile from LVN detection.

data.name: LVN_TailFilterRangePct_Description
data.value: Excludes low-volume tail areas near the high and low of the profile from LVN detection, using a percentage of the profile range. (0 = disabled).

data.name: ProfitColorDescription
data.value: Color used to display profitable trades.

data.name: LossColorDescription
data.value: Color used to display losing trades.

data.name: LabelDistanceDescription
data.value: Vertical spacing between trade markers and labels (px).

data.name: LabelXAnchorDescription
data.value: Defines the horizontal reference used to position trade labels.

data.name: LabelModeDescription
data.value: Selects how trade labels are displayed.

data.name: TradeLineDescription
data.value: Draws a line connecting entry and exit markers.

data.name: TradeDetailsDescription
data.value: Shows trade details when hovering markers or labels.
